### User *(2026-03-20T22:51:04.026Z)*

**Tool result**
```
    73→## Step 1 — Query patterns
    74→
    75→### Pattern A — Title + author, text-indexed (primary)
    76→
    77→Used when: author GND URI is available AND `contains-word` is confirmed (recon 0.2).
    78→
    79→```sparql
    80→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
    81→
    82→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel ?score WHERE {
    83→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork }
    84→  ?work a ?wtype .
    85→  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .
    86→  ?work gndo:firstAuthor <{author_gnd_uri}> .
    87→  ?prefLabel contains-word "{title_tokens}"
    88→  BIND(score(?prefLabel) AS ?score)
    89→}
    90→ORDER BY DESC(?score)
    91→TEXTLIMIT 5
    92→LIMIT 10
    93→```
    94→
    95→Replace `gndo:firstAuthor` with the predicate confirmed in recon 0.3.
    96→`TEXTLIMIT 5` caps QLever's internal text candidates for performance; `LIMIT 10` caps output rows.
    97→
    98→### Pattern B — Title only, text-indexed
    99→
   100→Used when: no author GND URI is available.
   101→UNION catches cases where the DDB title matches a GND variant name rather than the preferred form.
   102→
   103→```sparql
   104→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   105→
   106→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel ?varLabel ?score WHERE {
   107→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork }
   108→  ?work a ?wtype .
   109→  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .
   110→  {
   111→    ?prefLabel contains-word "{title_tokens}"
   112→    BIND(?prefLabel AS ?varLabel)
   113→    BIND(score(?prefLabel) AS ?score)
   114→  } UNION {
   115→    ?work gndo:variantNameForTheWork ?alt .
   116→    ?alt contains-word "{title_tokens}"
   117→    BIND(?alt AS ?varLabel)
   118→    BIND(score(?alt) AS ?score)
   119→  }
   120→}
   121→ORDER BY DESC(?score)
   122→TEXTLIMIT 5
   123→LIMIT 10
   124→```
   125→
   126→### Pattern C — FILTER fallback (if `contains-word` not available on the predicate)
   127→
   128→Exact-match only; normalized title (diacritics stripped, lowercased) maximizes recall.
   129→
   130→```sparql
   131→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   132→
   133→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel WHERE {
   134→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork }
   135→  ?work a ?wtype .
   136→  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .
   137→  FILTER(LCASE(STR(?prefLabel)) = "{normalized_title}")
   138→}
   139→LIMIT 10
   140→```
   141→
   142→---
   143→
   144→## Step 2 — Title token preparation
   145→
   146→QLever `contains-word` is word-based, not phrase-based by default.
   147→
   148→**Tokenization rules:**
   149→1. Unicode NFC normalize
   150→2. Lowercase
   151→3. Strip diacritics (test both forms — QLever may or may not handle Umlauts natively; see open questions)
   152→4. Remove stopwords: `der, die, das, ein, eine, von, und, zu, im, in, an, auf, für, mit, bei, dem, den`



```

### Assistant *(2026-03-20T23:05:22.160Z)*

Here are all classes with "Work" or "Werk" in name or label:

**Root class**

| Class URI | Label (en) | Label (de) | Subclass of | MARC21 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `:Work` | Work | Werk | `:AuthorityResource` | `079 $b=u`, `079 $v=wit` |

**Direct subclasses of `:Work`**

| Class URI | Label (en) | Label (de) | MARC21 |
|---|---|---|---|
| `:CollectiveManuscript` | Collective manuscript | Sammelhandschrift | `079 $v=wil` |
| `:Expression` | Expression | Expression | `079 $v=wie` |
| `:Manuscript` | Manuscript | Schriftdenkmal | `079 $v=wis` |
| `:MusicalWork` | Musical work | Werk der Musik | `079 $v=wim` |
| `:ProvenanceCharacteristic` | Provenance characteristic | Provenienzmerkmal | `079 $v=wip` |
| `:VersionOfAMusicalWork` | Version of a musical work | Fassung eines Werks der Musik | `079 $v=wif` |

**Near-miss (contains "Werk" in German label but not in Work hierarchy)**

| Class URI | Label (en) | Label (de) | Subclass of |
|---|---|---|---|
| `:BuildingOrMemorial` | Building or memorial | Bauwerk oder Denkmal | `:PlaceOrGeographicName` |

---

Two things stand out for the plan:

1. **`:Expression` is a subclass of `:Work`** in this ontology — not a separate FRBR-style level. This means `:Expression` entities will be caught by queries that include `VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work }` only if you also add `:Expression` explicitly. Worth deciding whether DDB Werk links should target `:Work` proper, or `:Work` + all subclasses.

2. **The `VALUES ?wtype` in Patterns A/B** currently has `gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork`. The complete set to consider is those six subclasses. For DDB literary/bibliographic items the most relevant are `:Work`, `:MusicalWork`, `:Manuscript`, `:CollectiveManuscript`.

Should I update gnd-linking-plan.md with the full subclass list and the `:Expression` note?

---

### User *(2026-03-20T23:07:30.701Z)*

No, only include direct conceptual match with IFLA "Work". Manuscript would also be considered as a Work in IFLA, right?

---

### Assistant *(2026-03-20T23:08:03.338Z)*

Yes. In IFLA LRM (and FRBR before it), a **Schriftdenkmal** — a historical manuscript as a unique intellectual creation (Nibelungenlied, Beowulf, etc.) — is a Work: the distinct intellectual or artistic creation, regardless of the physical carrier. The GND `:Manuscript` class maps cleanly to IFLA Work.

The full mapping:

| GND class | IFLA LRM equivalent | Include? |
|---|---|---|
| `:Work` | Work (LRM-E2) | Yes |
| `:MusicalWork` | Work (LRM-E2) | Yes |
| `:Manuscript` | Work (LRM-E2) — unique intellectual creation | Yes |
| `:CollectiveManuscript` | Manifestation (LRM-E4) — container of multiple works | No |
| `:Expression` | Expression (LRM-E3) — realization of a Work | No |
| `:VersionOfAMusicalWork` | Expression (LRM-E3) — specific arrangement/version | No |
| `:ProvenanceCharacteristic` | No WEMI equivalent — ownership marks on items | No |

`:CollectiveManuscript` is a Sammelhandschrift — a physical collection binding together multiple works, which puts it at the Manifestation level. `:VersionOfAMusicalWork` and `:Expression` are both LRM-E3 — one level below Work.

Updating the plan:

    73→## Step 1 — Query patterns
    74→

    77→| **A** — Title + author | `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` (text search); `gndo:firstAuthor` (constraint) | Author URI narrows the candidate set before text matching; highest precision. Use when author GND URI is available and text index is confirmed (recon 0.2–0.3). |
    78→| **B** — Title only | `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` (preferred); `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` (UNION fallback) | No author URI available. UNION over variant names increases recall for cases where the DDB title matches a GND alternative form rather than the preferred label. Use when text index is confirmed but no author URI. |
    79→| **C** — FILTER fallback | `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` (exact string match) | `contains-word` unavailable or not indexed for the predicate (determined by recon 0.2). Exact/normalized string comparison only; no relevance ranking. Lower recall than A/B. |
    80→

    24→## Step 0 — Recon queries (run once before implementation)

    28→### 0.1 Work type coverage
    29→
    30→Determine which GND classes to include in `VALUES ?wtype`:
    31→
    32→```sparql
    33→SELECT ?type (COUNT(?w) AS ?n) WHERE {
    34→  ?w a ?type .
    35→  FILTER(STRSTARTS(STR(?type), "https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#"))
    36→}
    37→GROUP BY ?type ORDER BY DESC(?n) LIMIT 20
    38→```
    39→
    40→Expected results include `gndo:Work`, `gndo:MusicalWork`, `gndo:SeriesOfConferencePublications`. Record which to include.
    41→
    42→### 0.2 Confirm text index covers `preferredNameForTheWork`
    43→
    44→```sparql
    45→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
    46→SELECT ?w ?label ?score WHERE {
    47→  ?w a gndo:Work .
    48→  ?w gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?label .

    51→}

    53→```

    65→}
    66→LIMIT 10

```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/gnd-linking-plan.md",
  "old_string": "### 0.1 Work type coverage\n\nDetermine which GND classes to include in `VALUES ?wtype`:\n\n```sparql\nSELECT ?type (COUNT(?w) AS ?n) WHERE {\n  ?w a ?type .\n  FILTER(STRSTARTS(STR(?type), \"https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#\"))\n}\nGROUP BY ?type ORDER BY DESC(?n) LIMIT 20\n```\n\nExpected results include `gndo:Work`, `gndo:MusicalWork`, `gndo:SeriesOfConferencePublications`. Record which to include.",
  "new_string": "### 0.1 Work type coverage\n\nIFLA LRM defines a Work (LRM-E2) as \"a distinct intellectual or artistic creation\" — the abstract entity at the top of the WEMI hierarchy, independent of any particular realization or carrier. The GND ontology (`gnd_20251218.ttl`) models six subclasses of `gndo:Work`. Only three correspond to the IFLA LRM Work concept:\n\n| GND class | Label (en / de) | MARC21 | IFLA LRM | Include in queries |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `gndo:Work` | Work / Werk | `079 $v=wit` | LRM-E2 Work — direct match | **Yes** |\n| `gndo:MusicalWork` | Musical work / Werk der Musik | `079 $v=wim` | LRM-E2 Work — a musical composition as distinct creative entity | **Yes** |\n| `gndo:Manuscript` | Manuscript / Schriftdenkmal | `079 $v=wis` | LRM-E2 Work — a unique historical manuscript (e.g. Nibelungenlied) is the distinct intellectual creation itself | **Yes** |\n| `gndo:Expression` | Expression / Expression | `079 $v=wie` | LRM-E3 Expression — the realization of a Work in a specific linguistic or notational form; one level below Work in WEMI | No |\n| `gndo:VersionOfAMusicalWork` | Version of a musical work / Fassung eines Werks der Musik | `079 $v=wif` | LRM-E3 Expression — a specific arrangement or version is a realization of the Work, not a new Work (unless substantially transformed) | No |\n| `gndo:CollectiveManuscript` | Collective manuscript / Sammelhandschrift | `079 $v=wil` | LRM-E4 Manifestation — a bound collection of multiple works; the container, not the intellectual creation | No |\n| `gndo:ProvenanceCharacteristic` | Provenance characteristic / Provenienzmerkmal | `079 $v=wip` | No WEMI equivalent — ownership marks and traces left in/on physical items; not a creative entity | No |\n\n```sparql\nSELECT ?type (COUNT(?w) AS ?n) WHERE {\n  ?w a ?type .\n  FILTER(STRSTARTS(STR(?type), \"https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#\"))\n}\nGROUP BY ?type ORDER BY DESC(?n) LIMIT 20\n```\n\nRun to confirm population counts before finalising `VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }`."
}
```

```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/gnd-linking-plan.md",
  "old_string": "Script: `scripts/link_gnd_works.py`\nReplaces: lobid-gnd REST API approach (previously planned but endpoint was TBD)",
  "new_string": "Script: `scripts/link_gnd_works.py`\nReplaces: lobid-gnd REST API approach (previously planned but endpoint was TBD)\n\n`link_gnd_works.py` is an **offline ingest step**, not a runtime component. It runs once (or on re-ingest) during Phase 0 to enrich ProvidedCHO records with GND Werk URIs. Its output feeds mocho → QLever → the GeMeA API. The GND linking results are ultimately exposed through the API's `/work/{id}` endpoint and SPARQL proxy — `link_gnd_works.py` itself never serves live requests. Design constraints follow from this: throughput and batch efficiency matter; latency per query does not."
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-03-20T23:21:12.302Z)*

`https://d-nb.info/gnd/118607359` is **Friedrich Schiller** (Johann Christoph Friedrich von Schiller, 1759–1805) — poet, playwright, philosopher.

Also worth flagging: recon query 0.3 is inverted. As written it queries predicates *from* Schiller outward — that returns things like `gndo:relatedPerson`, not `gndo:firstAuthor`. To find the predicate that links a Work *to* an author URI, the query should be the other direction:

```sparql
PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
SELECT ?work ?pred WHERE {
  ?work ?pred <https://d-nb.info/gnd/118607359> .
  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
  ?work a ?wtype .
}
LIMIT 10
```

Should I update 0.3 in the plan with the corrected query?

### Assistant *(2026-03-20T23:27:09.796Z)*

Key finding: `:firstAuthor` has `rdfs:subPropertyOf :author` (line 2248). The full picture:

| Property | Label (en/de) | `rdfs:subPropertyOf` | MARC21 | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `gndo:author` | Author / Verfasser | RDA `P60434` | `$4=auta` | Textual works; primary creator |
| `gndo:firstAuthor` | First author / Erste Verfasserschaft | **`:author`**, RDA `P60434` | `$4=aut1` | Subproperty of `author`; single/lead author |
| `gndo:creator` | Creator / Urheber | — (none) | `$4=urhe` | General; no subproperty relation to `author` |
| `gndo:poet` | Poet / Dichter | RDA `P60477` | `$4=dich` | Words of non-dramatic musical works |
| `gndo:composer` | Composer / Komponist | RDA `P60426` | `$4=koma` | Musical works |
| `gndo:artist` | Artist / Künstler | — | — | Visual/plastic works |

Critical implication for Pattern A: **SPARQL does not infer `rdfs:subPropertyOf` without a reasoner**, so `?work gndo:author <uri>` will miss triples using `gndo:firstAuthor`. Both must be queried explicitly. Now updating the plan:

---

  "new_string": "**Conclusion:** The dominant predicate is `gndo:author` (8/10 results). `gndo:poet` appears as a role-specific variant (Goethe wrote lyrics for musical works); `gndo:relatedPerson` is too broad for author linking.\n\nFrom `gnd_20251218.ttl`, the relevant author-role property hierarchy is:\n\n| Property | Label (en/de) | `rdfs:subPropertyOf` | MARC21 | Scope |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `gndo:author` | Author / Verfasser | RDA `P60434` | `$4=auta` | Textual works; primary creator |\n| `gndo:firstAuthor` | First author / Erste Verfasserschaft | **`gndo:author`**, RDA `P60434` | `$4=aut1` | Subproperty of `author`; single/lead author |\n| `gndo:creator` | Creator / Urheber | — | `$4=urhe` | General; no subproperty relation to `author` |\n| `gndo:poet` | Poet / Dichter | RDA `P60477` | `$4=dich` | Words of non-dramatic musical works |\n| `gndo:composer` | Composer / Komponist | RDA `P60426` | `$4=koma` | Musical works |\n\n**SPARQL does not infer `rdfs:subPropertyOf` without a reasoner.** A query constraining `gndo:author` will miss triples that use `gndo:firstAuthor`, even though `firstAuthor` is a subproperty of `author`. Pattern A must explicitly cover both using `VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor }`. `gndo:poet` and `gndo:composer` should be added for coverage of MusicalWork records."

  "old_string": "### Pattern A — Title + author, text-indexed (primary)\n\nUsed when: author GND URI is available AND `contains-word` is confirmed (recon 0.2).\n\n```sparql\nPREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>\n\nSELECT ?work ?prefLabel ?score WHERE {\n  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }\n  ?work a ?wtype .\n  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .\n  ?work gndo:author <{author_gnd_uri}> .\n  ?prefLabel contains-word \"{title_tokens}\"\n  BIND(score(?prefLabel) AS ?score)\n}\nORDER BY DESC(?score)\nTEXTLIMIT 5\nLIMIT 10\n```\n\nReplace `gndo:firstAuthor` with the predicate confirmed in recon 0.3.\n`TEXTLIMIT 5` caps QLever's internal text candidates for performance; `LIMIT 10` caps output rows.",
  "new_string": "### Pattern A — Title + author, text-indexed (primary)\n\nUsed when: author GND URI is available AND `contains-word` is confirmed (recon 0.2).\n\n`gndo:firstAuthor` is a subproperty of `gndo:author` but SPARQL does not infer this without a reasoner — both must be listed explicitly. `gndo:poet` and `gndo:composer` cover musical and lyrical works.\n\n```sparql\nPREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>\n\nSELECT ?work ?prefLabel ?score WHERE {\n  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }\n  VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }\n  ?work a ?wtype .\n  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .\n  ?work ?authorPred <{author_gnd_uri}> .\n  ?prefLabel contains-word \"{title_tokens}\"\n  BIND(score(?prefLabel) AS ?score)\n}\nORDER BY DESC(?score)\nTEXTLIMIT 5\nLIMIT 10\n```\n\n`TEXTLIMIT 5` caps QLever's internal text candidates for performance; `LIMIT 10` caps output rows."
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-03-20T23:35:48.970Z)*

The original query scans all triples to discover types dynamically — too expensive on a full GND dataset. Since we already know the Work subclasses from the ontology, constrain with `VALUES` upfront:

### Assistant *(2026-03-20T23:35:53.027Z)*

**Tool call:** `Edit`
```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/gnd-linking-plan.md",
  "old_string": "```sparql\nSELECT ?type (COUNT(?w) AS ?n) WHERE {\n  ?w a ?type .\n  FILTER(STRSTARTS(STR(?type), \"https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#\"))\n}\nGROUP BY ?type ORDER BY DESC(?n) LIMIT 20\n```\n\nRun to confirm population counts before finalising `VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }`.",
  "new_string": "```sparql\nPREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>\nSELECT ?type (COUNT(?w) AS ?n) WHERE {\n  VALUES ?type {\n    gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript\n    gndo:Expression gndo:VersionOfAMusicalWork\n    gndo:CollectiveManuscript gndo:ProvenanceCharacteristic\n  }\n  ?w a ?type .\n}\nGROUP BY ?type ORDER BY DESC(?n)\n```\n\nConstraining with `VALUES` avoids a full dataset type scan and lets the endpoint use per-type indexes. The original open-ended query caused a 504 Gateway Timeout. Run to confirm population counts before finalising `VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }`."
}
```

The three IFLA LRM Work-equivalent classes cover 580,284 entities (242,333 + 330,818 + 7,133). The four excluded classes are either IFLA Expression-level (`Expression`, `VersionOfAMusicalWork`), have no WEMI equivalent (`ProvenanceCharacteristic`), or are absent from this dataset (`CollectiveManuscript`)."

   155→|---|---|---|
   156→| **A** — Title + author | `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` (text search); `gndo:author`, `gndo:firstAuthor`, `gndo:poet`, `gndo:composer` (constraint via VALUES) | Author URI narrows the candidate set before text matching; highest precision. Use when author GND URI is available and text index is confirmed (recon 0.2–0.3). |
   157→| **B** — Title only | `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` (preferred); `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` (UNION fallback) | No author URI available. UNION over variant names increases recall for cases where the DDB title matches a GND alternative form rather than the preferred label. Use when text index is confirmed but no author URI. |
   158→| **C** — FILTER fallback | `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` (exact string match) | `contains-word` unavailable or not indexed for the predicate (determined by recon 0.2). Exact/normalized string comparison only; no relevance ranking. Lower recall than A/B. |
   159→
   160→### Pattern A — Title + author, text-indexed (primary)
   161→
   162→Used when: author GND URI is available AND `contains-word` is confirmed (recon 0.2).
   163→
   164→`gndo:firstAuthor` is a subproperty of `gndo:author` but SPARQL does not infer this without a reasoner — both must be listed explicitly. `gndo:poet` and `gndo:composer` cover musical and lyrical works.
   165→
   166→```sparql
   167→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   168→
   169→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel ?score WHERE {
   170→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
   171→  VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }
   172→  ?work a ?wtype .
   173→  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .
   174→  ?work ?authorPred <{author_gnd_uri}> .
   175→  ?prefLabel contains-word "{title_tokens}"
   176→  BIND(score(?prefLabel) AS ?score)
   177→}
   178→ORDER BY DESC(?score)
   179→TEXTLIMIT 5
   180→LIMIT 10
   181→```
   182→
   183→`TEXTLIMIT 5` caps QLever's internal text candidates for performance; `LIMIT 10` caps output rows.
   184→
   185→### Pattern B — Title only, text-indexed
   186→
   187→Used when: no author GND URI is available.
   188→UNION catches cases where the DDB title matches a GND variant name rather than the preferred form.
   189→
   190→```sparql
   191→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   192→
   193→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel ?varLabel ?score WHERE {
   194→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
   195→  ?work a ?wtype .
   196→  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .
   197→  {
   198→    ?prefLabel contains-word "{title_tokens}"
   199→    BIND(?prefLabel AS ?varLabel)
   200→    BIND(score(?prefLabel) AS ?score)
   201→  } UNION {
   202→    ?work gndo:variantNameForTheWork ?alt .
   203→    ?alt contains-word "{title_tokens}"
   204→    BIND(?alt AS ?varLabel)
   205→    BIND(score(?alt) AS ?score)
   206→  }
   207→}
   208→ORDER BY DESC(?score)
   209→TEXTLIMIT 5
   210→LIMIT 10
   211→```
   212→
   213→### Pattern C — FILTER fallback (if `contains-word` not available on the predicate)
   214→

### User *(2026-03-20T23:44:50.474Z)*

Let's talk about Step 2 now: Tokenization rules. what is NFC tokenization rules

---

### Assistant *(2026-03-20T23:45:06.239Z)*

NFC is not a tokenization rule — it's a **Unicode normalization form**. The two concepts are separate and the note conflates them.

**Unicode Normalization Form C (NFC)** — defined in Unicode Standard Annex #15 — transforms a string into *Canonical Decomposition followed by Canonical Composition*. In practice for German bibliographic data it means:

- `ä` stored as `a` + combining diaeresis (two code points, NFD form) → composed back to `ä` (one code point, NFC)
- Ensures that two strings that look identical but use different Unicode representations compare as equal

It is a **pre-processing step before tokenization**, not a tokenization rule itself. Its purpose is to guarantee consistent code-point representation so that downstream operations (lowercasing, diacritic stripping, splitting on whitespace) behave predictably regardless of how the source string was encoded.

**Tokenization** is the separate step of splitting the normalized string into tokens — typically on whitespace and punctuation boundaries.

The note should separate these two concerns. The correct order is:

1. Unicode NFC normalize — *normalization* (ensures consistent encoding)
2. Lowercase
3. Strip diacritics — *normalization* (optional; depends on endpoint behavior)
4. Split on whitespace/punctuation — *tokenization* (this is the actual tokenization step)
5. Remove stopwords — *filtering*
6. Select distinctive tokens — *filtering*

Steps 1–3 are normalization; step 4 is tokenization; steps 5–6 are filtering. Should I update the note to reflect this distinction?

   265→**Criterion 2 — Low collision frequency.** Prefer tokens that appear in few GND Works. Some words are not stopwords but are so common in bibliographic titles that they add almost no discriminating power:
   266→
   267→```python
   268→GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS = {
   269→    "briefe", "gedichte", "werke", "schriften", "geschichte",
   270→    "bericht", "katalog", "sammlung", "aufsätze", "texte",
   271→    "band", "teil", "heft", "nummer", "jahrgang",
   272→}
   273→```
   274→
   275→Example: *"Gedichte"* alone returns thousands of GND Works. *"faust"* alone is sufficient with an author URI.
   276→
   277→This list should be derived empirically from a frequency query against the endpoint, not hardcoded blindly — run after recon 0.1.
   278→
   279→**Decision rule:**
   280→
   281→```python
   282→def select_tokens(tokens: list[str]) -> list[str]:
   283→    # tokens: already stopword-filtered, lowercased, diacritics-stripped
   284→    distinctive = [t for t in tokens if t not in GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS and len(t) > 3]

   243→## Step 2 — Title token preparation
   244→

   246→

   254→### Distinctive token selection
   255→
   256→**Underlying principle:** `contains-word` with multiple tokens is AND semantics — each additional token makes the query more restrictive. Candidate retrieval should maximize recall (get the right GND Work somewhere in the top-10); precision is handled by post-retrieval string scoring in Step 3. The goal is the minimum set of tokens that identifies the work without over-constraining.

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/gnd-linking-plan.md",
  "old_string": "## Step 2 — Title token preparation\n\nQLever `contains-word` is word-based, not phrase-based by default.\n\n**Tokenization rules:**\n1. Unicode NFC normalize\n2. Lowercase\n3. Strip diacritics (test both forms — QLever may or may not handle Umlauts natively; see open questions)\n4. Remove stopwords: `der, die, das, ein, eine, von, und, zu, im, in, an, auf, für, mit, bei, dem, den`\n5. For multi-word titles: select 2–3 distinctive tokens (see below)",
  "new_string": "## Step 2 — Title token preparation\n\nQLever `contains-word` is word-based, not phrase-based by default. Title preparation has three distinct phases: normalization, tokenization, and filtering.\n\n### Normalization (pre-tokenization)\n\n1. **Unicode NFC normalize** — Unicode Standard Annex #15, Normalization Form C: Canonical Decomposition followed by Canonical Composition. Ensures consistent code-point representation before any string operation. Example: `a` + combining diaeresis (two code points, NFD) → `ä` (one code point, NFC). Required so that lowercasing, diacritic stripping, and string comparison behave predictably regardless of source encoding.\n2. **Lowercase** — case-fold the NFC-normalized string.\n3. **Strip diacritics** — decompose (NFD) then remove combining characters (`unicodedata.category(c) == 'Mn'`). Maps `ä→a`, `ü→u`, `ö→o`, `ß→ss`. Note: test whether the GND endpoint requires this — if the text index is accent-insensitive, stripping may reduce rather than increase recall.\n\n### Tokenization\n\n4. **Split on whitespace and punctuation** — split the normalized string into individual word tokens.\n\n### Filtering\n\n5. **Remove stopwords:** `der, die, das, ein, eine, von, und, zu, im, in, an, auf, für, mit, bei, dem, den`\n6. **Select 2–3 distinctive tokens** (see below)"
}
```

### User *(2026-03-20T23:51:41.863Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→# GeMeA — GND Werk Linking via QLever DNB Endpoint
     2→
     3→Script: `scripts/link_gnd_works.py`
     4→Replaces: lobid-gnd REST API approach (previously planned but endpoint was TBD)
     5→
     6→`link_gnd_works.py` is an **offline ingest step**, not a runtime component. It runs once (or on re-ingest) during Phase 0 to enrich ProvidedCHO records with GND Werk URIs. Its output feeds mocho → QLever → the GeMeA API. The GND linking results are ultimately exposed through the API's `/work/{id}` endpoint and SPARQL proxy — `link_gnd_works.py` itself never serves live requests. Design constraints follow from this: throughput and batch efficiency matter; latency per query does not.
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Knowledge base index
    11→
    12→**Description:** DNB Bibliography and GND (Gemeinsame Normdatei) (`authorities-gnd_lds`, `dnb-all_lds` version 23.02.2026) — updated monthly
    13→
    14→| Metric | Value |
    15→|---|---|
    16→| Triples | 1,242,168,767 |
    17→| Subjects | 186,556,644 |
    18→| Predicates | 591 |
    19→| Objects | 457,761,162 |
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## Endpoint and HTTP API
    24→
    25→**Endpoint:** `https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd`
    26→**Method:** `POST`
    27→**Body:** `query=<URL-encoded SPARQL>` (`application/x-www-form-urlencoded`)
    28→**Accept:** `application/sparql-results+json`
    29→
    30→QLever instance settings:
    31→- Languages: `en`
    32→- Default max rows: 100
    33→- Default mode: 3 — SPARQL & context-sensitive entities
    34→
    35→QLever-specific keywords used: `contains-word`, `textlimit`, `score()`
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## Step 0 — Recon queries (run once before implementation)
    40→
    41→Three things to verify before writing production queries.
    42→
    43→### 0.1 Work type coverage
    44→
    45→IFLA LRM defines a Work (LRM-E2) as "a distinct intellectual or artistic creation" — the abstract entity at the top of the WEMI hierarchy, independent of any particular realization or carrier. The GND ontology (`gnd_20251218.ttl`) models six subclasses of `gndo:Work`. Only three correspond to the IFLA LRM Work concept:
    46→
    47→| GND class | Label (en / de) | MARC21 | IFLA LRM | Include in queries |
    48→|---|---|---|---|---|
    49→| `gndo:Work` | Work / Werk | `079 $v=wit` | LRM-E2 Work — direct match | **Yes** |
    50→| `gndo:MusicalWork` | Musical work / Werk der Musik | `079 $v=wim` | LRM-E2 Work — a musical composition as distinct creative entity | **Yes** |
    51→| `gndo:Manuscript` | Manuscript / Schriftdenkmal | `079 $v=wis` | LRM-E2 Work — a unique historical manuscript (e.g. Nibelungenlied) is the distinct intellectual creation itself | **Yes** |
    52→| `gndo:Expression` | Expression / Expression | `079 $v=wie` | LRM-E3 Expression — the realization of a Work in a specific linguistic or notational form; one level below Work in WEMI | No |
    53→| `gndo:VersionOfAMusicalWork` | Version of a musical work / Fassung eines Werks der Musik | `079 $v=wif` | LRM-E3 Expression — a specific arrangement or version is a realization of the Work, not a new Work (unless substantially transformed) | No |
    54→| `gndo:CollectiveManuscript` | Collective manuscript / Sammelhandschrift | `079 $v=wil` | LRM-E4 Manifestation — a bound collection of multiple works; the container, not the intellectual creation | No |
    55→| `gndo:ProvenanceCharacteristic` | Provenance characteristic / Provenienzmerkmal | `079 $v=wip` | No WEMI equivalent — ownership marks and traces left in/on physical items; not a creative entity | No |
    56→
    57→```sparql
    58→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
    59→SELECT ?type (COUNT(?w) AS ?n) WHERE {
    60→  VALUES ?type {
    61→    gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript
    62→    gndo:Expression gndo:VersionOfAMusicalWork
    63→    gndo:CollectiveManuscript gndo:ProvenanceCharacteristic
    64→  }
    65→  ?w a ?type .
    66→}
    67→GROUP BY ?type ORDER BY DESC(?n)
    68→```
    69→
    70→Constraining with `VALUES` avoids a full dataset type scan and lets the endpoint use per-type indexes. The original open-ended query caused a 504 Gateway Timeout.
    71→
    72→**Results:**
    73→
    74→| ?type | ?n |
    75→|---|---|
    76→| MusicalWork | 330,818 |
    77→| Work | 242,333 |
    78→| ProvenanceCharacteristic | 10,080 |
    79→| Manuscript | 7,133 |
    80→| Expression | 5,766 |
    81→| VersionOfAMusicalWork | 2,905 |
    82→| CollectiveManuscript | — (no results; zero entities in dataset) |
    83→
    84→**Conclusion:** `VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }` is confirmed. The three IFLA LRM Work-equivalent classes cover 580,284 entities (242,333 + 330,818 + 7,133). The four excluded classes are either IFLA Expression-level (`Expression`, `VersionOfAMusicalWork`), have no WEMI equivalent (`ProvenanceCharacteristic`), or are absent from this dataset (`CollectiveManuscript`).
    85→
    86→### 0.2 Confirm text index covers `preferredNameForTheWork`
    87→
    88→```sparql
    89→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
    90→SELECT ?w ?label ?score WHERE {
    91→  ?w a gndo:Work .
    92→  ?w gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?label .
    93→  ?label contains-word "faust"
    94→  BIND(score(?label) AS ?score)
    95→}
    96→ORDER BY DESC(?score) LIMIT 5
    97→```
    98→
    99→**Result:** Parse error —
   100→```
   101→Invalid SPARQL query: Token "contains-word": mismatched input 'contains-word'
   102→expecting {'(', 'a', '^', '!', IRI_REF, PNAME_NS, PNAME_LN, VAR1, VAR2, PREFIX_LANGTAG}
   103→```
   104→
   105→The endpoint validates against the SPARQL 1.1 grammar; `contains-word` is QLever-specific syntax and is not available here. **Patterns A and B do not apply. Pattern C is the active query path.**
   106→
   107→### 0.3 Confirm author predicate name
   108→
   109→Query Works that link to Goethe (`https://d-nb.info/gnd/118540238`) to find the predicate used:
   110→
   111→```sparql
   112→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   113→SELECT ?work ?pred WHERE {
   114→  ?work ?pred <https://d-nb.info/gnd/118540238> .
   115→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
   116→  ?work a ?wtype .
   117→}
   118→LIMIT 10
   119→```
   120→
   121→**Results:**
   122→
   123→| ?work | ?pred |
   124→|---|---|
   125→| 4427937-1 | author |
   126→| 134483180X | relatedPerson |
   127→| 1367943906 | author |
   128→| 1345022085 | author |
   129→| 1367911087 | author |
   130→| 134558573X | author |
   131→| 1345758731 | poet |
   132→| 1346874786 | author |
   133→| 1347120017 | author |
   134→| 1347121307 | author |
   135→
   136→**Conclusion:** The dominant predicate is `gndo:author` (8/10 results). `gndo:poet` appears as a role-specific variant (Goethe wrote lyrics for musical works); `gndo:relatedPerson` is too broad for author linking.
   137→
   138→From `gnd_20251218.ttl`, the relevant author-role property hierarchy is:
   139→
   140→| Property | Label (en/de) | `rdfs:subPropertyOf` | MARC21 | Scope |
   141→|---|---|---|---|---|
   142→| `gndo:author` | Author / Verfasser | RDA `P60434` | `$4=auta` | Textual works; primary creator |
   143→| `gndo:firstAuthor` | First author / Erste Verfasserschaft | **`gndo:author`**, RDA `P60434` | `$4=aut1` | Subproperty of `author`; single/lead author |
   144→| `gndo:creator` | Creator / Urheber | — | `$4=urhe` | General; no subproperty relation to `author` |
   145→| `gndo:poet` | Poet / Dichter | RDA `P60477` | `$4=dich` | Words of non-dramatic musical works |
   146→| `gndo:composer` | Composer / Komponist | RDA `P60426` | `$4=koma` | Musical works |
   147→
   148→**SPARQL does not infer `rdfs:subPropertyOf` without a reasoner.** A query constraining `gndo:author` will miss triples that use `gndo:firstAuthor`, even though `firstAuthor` is a subproperty of `author`. Pattern A must explicitly cover both using `VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor }`. `gndo:poet` and `gndo:composer` should be added for coverage of MusicalWork records.
   149→
   150→---
   151→
   152→## Step 1 — Query patterns
   153→
   154→| Query pattern | Fields | Justification |
   155→|---|---|---|
   156→| **A** — Title + author | `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` (text search); `gndo:author`, `gndo:firstAuthor`, `gndo:poet`, `gndo:composer` (constraint via VALUES) | Author URI narrows the candidate set before text matching; highest precision. Use when author GND URI is available and text index is confirmed (recon 0.2–0.3). |
   157→| **B** — Title only | `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` (preferred); `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` (UNION fallback) | No author URI available. UNION over variant names increases recall for cases where the DDB title matches a GND alternative form rather than the preferred label. Use when text index is confirmed but no author URI. |
   158→| **C** — FILTER fallback | `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` (exact string match) | `contains-word` unavailable or not indexed for the predicate (determined by recon 0.2). Exact/normalized string comparison only; no relevance ranking. Lower recall than A/B. |
   159→
   160→### Pattern A — Title + author, text-indexed (primary)
   161→
   162→Used when: author GND URI is available AND `contains-word` is confirmed (recon 0.2).
   163→
   164→`gndo:firstAuthor` is a subproperty of `gndo:author` but SPARQL does not infer this without a reasoner — both must be listed explicitly. `gndo:poet` and `gndo:composer` cover musical and lyrical works.
   165→
   166→```sparql
   167→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   168→
   169→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel ?score WHERE {
   170→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
   171→  VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }
   172→  ?work a ?wtype .
   173→  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .
   174→  ?work ?authorPred <{author_gnd_uri}> .
   175→  ?prefLabel contains-word "{title_tokens}"
   176→  BIND(score(?prefLabel) AS ?score)
   177→}
   178→ORDER BY DESC(?score)
   179→TEXTLIMIT 5
   180→LIMIT 10
   181→```
   182→
   183→`TEXTLIMIT 5` caps QLever's internal text candidates for performance; `LIMIT 10` caps output rows.
   184→
   185→### Pattern B — Title only, text-indexed
   186→
   187→Used when: no author GND URI is available.
   188→UNION catches cases where the DDB title matches a GND variant name rather than the preferred form.
   189→
   190→```sparql
   191→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   192→
   193→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel ?varLabel ?score WHERE {
   194→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
   195→  ?work a ?wtype .
   196→  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .
   197→  {
   198→    ?prefLabel contains-word "{title_tokens}"
   199→    BIND(?prefLabel AS ?varLabel)
   200→    BIND(score(?prefLabel) AS ?score)
   201→  } UNION {
   202→    ?work gndo:variantNameForTheWork ?alt .
   203→    ?alt contains-word "{title_tokens}"
   204→    BIND(?alt AS ?varLabel)
   205→    BIND(score(?alt) AS ?score)
   206→  }
   207→}
   208→ORDER BY DESC(?score)
   209→TEXTLIMIT 5
   210→LIMIT 10
   211→```
   212→
   213→### Pattern C — FILTER (active query path)
   214→
   215→Exact-match only; normalized title (diacritics stripped, lowercased) maximizes recall.
   216→
   217→```sparql
   218→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   219→
   220→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel WHERE {
   221→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
   222→  ?work a ?wtype .
   223→  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .
   224→  FILTER(LCASE(STR(?prefLabel)) = "{normalized_title}")
   225→}
   226→LIMIT 10
   227→```
   228→
   229→### Conclusion from Step 0
   230→
   231→| Finding | Source | Impact on Step 1 |
   232→|---|---|---|
   233→| `contains-word` raises a SPARQL parse error | Step 0.2 | Patterns A and B are not available. **Pattern C is the sole active query path.** |
   234→| `VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }` covers 580,284 entities (242k + 331k + 7k) | Step 0.1 | Confirmed — use in all patterns |
   235→| Dominant author predicate is `gndo:author`; `gndo:firstAuthor` is a subproperty but SPARQL does not infer it | Step 0.3 | Pattern A must use `VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }` |
   236→| `gndo:poet` appears for lyrical/musical works (Goethe as lyricist) | Step 0.3 | Include `gndo:poet` in `VALUES ?authorPred` |
   237→| `CollectiveManuscript` has zero entities in this dataset | Step 0.1 | Confirmed exclusion; no impact on queries |
   238→
   239→Pattern C with author constraint (when author GND URI is available) becomes the effective Pattern A: same `VALUES ?authorPred` constraint, FILTER instead of `contains-word`.
   240→
   241→---
   242→
   243→## Step 2 — Title token preparation
   244→
   245→QLever `contains-word` is word-based, not phrase-based by default. Title preparation has three distinct phases: normalization, tokenization, and filtering.
   246→
   247→### Normalization (pre-tokenization)
   248→
   249→1. **Unicode NFC normalize** — Unicode Standard Annex #15, Normalization Form C: Canonical Decomposition followed by Canonical Composition. Ensures consistent code-point representation before any string operation. Example: `a` + combining diaeresis (two code points, NFD) → `ä` (one code point, NFC). Required so that lowercasing, diacritic stripping, and string comparison behave predictably regardless of source encoding.
   250→2. **Lowercase** — case-fold the NFC-normalized string.
   251→3. **Strip diacritics** — decompose (NFD) then remove combining characters (`unicodedata.category(c) == 'Mn'`). Maps `ä→a`, `ü→u`, `ö→o`, `ß→ss`. Note: test whether the GND endpoint requires this — if the text index is accent-insensitive, stripping may reduce rather than increase recall.
   252→
   253→### Tokenization
   254→
   255→4. **Split on whitespace and punctuation** — split the normalized string into individual word tokens.
   256→
   257→### Filtering
   258→
   259→5. **Remove stopwords:** `der, die, das, ein, eine, von, und, zu, im, in, an, auf, für, mit, bei, dem, den`
   260→6. **Select 2–3 distinctive tokens** (see below)
   261→
   262→### Distinctive token selection
   263→
   264→**Underlying principle:** `contains-word` with multiple tokens is AND semantics — each additional token makes the query more restrictive. Candidate retrieval should maximize recall (get the right GND Work somewhere in the top-10); precision is handled by post-retrieval string scoring in Step 3. The goal is the minimum set of tokens that identifies the work without over-constraining.
   265→
   266→**Criterion 1 — Semantic centrality.** Use tokens that are the essential content of the title, not modifiers or structural words:
   267→
   268→- **Keep:** nouns, proper nouns (place/person names embedded in titles), semantically unique adjectives
   269→- **Drop even if not in the stopword list:** articles, prepositions, ordinals (*erster, zweiter*), format/edition terms (*Band, Teil, Heft, Auflage, Bd., Nr.*)
   270→
   271→Example: *"Die Leiden des jungen Werthers"* → tokens `leiden werthers`; the proper noun alone (`werthers`) is sufficient with Pattern A.
   272→
   273→**Criterion 2 — Low collision frequency.** Prefer tokens that appear in few GND Works. Some words are not stopwords but are so common in bibliographic titles that they add almost no discriminating power:
   274→
   275→```python
   276→GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS = {
   277→    "briefe", "gedichte", "werke", "schriften", "geschichte",
   278→    "bericht", "katalog", "sammlung", "aufsätze", "texte",
   279→    "band", "teil", "heft", "nummer", "jahrgang",
   280→}
   281→```
   282→
   283→Example: *"Gedichte"* alone returns thousands of GND Works. *"faust"* alone is sufficient with an author URI.
   284→
   285→This list should be derived empirically from a frequency query against the endpoint, not hardcoded blindly — run after recon 0.1.
   286→
   287→**Decision rule:**
   288→
   289→```python
   290→def select_tokens(tokens: list[str]) -> list[str]:
   291→    # tokens: already stopword-filtered, lowercased, diacritics-stripped
   292→    distinctive = [t for t in tokens if t not in GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS and len(t) > 3]
   293→    return distinctive[:3] if len(distinctive) >= 2 else tokens[:3]
   294→```
   295→
   296→`len(t) > 3` also drops short high-frequency fragments not caught by the stopword list.
   297→
   298→**Why 2–3 tokens specifically:**
   299→
   300→- 1 token: usually sufficient with Pattern A (author constraint already narrows heavily); sometimes sufficient for highly distinctive titles in Pattern B
   301→- 2–3 tokens: the sweet spot for Pattern B — enough to discriminate, low risk of AND-excluding the right Work due to GND/DDB title variation
   302→- >3 tokens: increasing risk of false negatives when GND's preferred form differs from DDB's (e.g., GND has *"Faust. Der Tragödie erster Teil"* but DDB has *"Faust: Erster Teil"* — four tokens from the DDB form likely miss the GND entry)
   303→
   304→**Multi-word handling:**
   305→- Short (1–3 tokens after filtering): pass all remaining tokens
   306→- Long titles: apply `select_tokens()` above
   307→- High-ambiguity titles (single generic word like *Briefe*, *Gedichte* with no distinctive co-tokens): require author constraint (Pattern A); skip title-only search (Pattern B) if no author URI is available
   308→
   309→---
   310→
   311→## Step 3 — Post-retrieval scoring
   312→
   313→QLever's `score()` is used only to rank candidates before string comparison. Match type is assigned in Python:
   314→
   315→| Condition | `match_type` | Triple predicate |
   316→|---|---|---|
   317→| `prefLabel == extracted_title` (case-insensitive) | `exact` | `skos:exactMatch` |
   318→| Normalized match (strip diacritics + punctuation) | `normalized` | `skos:exactMatch` |
   319→| Levenshtein distance ≤ 2 to `prefLabel` or any `variantNameForTheWork` | `fuzzy` | `skos:closeMatch` |
   320→| No candidate above threshold | `unresolved` | — |
   321→
   322→`match_confidence` = normalized edit similarity to `prefLabel` (not QLever score).
   323→
   324→This logic is unchanged from the original plan in `gnd-title-extraction.md`.
   325→
   326→### Predicate justification
   327→
   328→**`skos:exactMatch`** (exact and normalized matches) — asserts two concepts are "an exact match" and can be used interchangeably for all retrieval purposes, without the OWL identity entailments of `owl:sameAs` (Miles & Bechhofer, *SKOS Simple Knowledge Organization System Reference*, W3C Recommendation, 2009, §10). Semantically appropriate here: a DDB ProvidedCHO and a GND Werk URI are not the same individual (CHO = catalog record for a manifestation; Werk URI = authority record for an abstract work), so asserting OWL individual identity would be incorrect.
   329→
   330→**`skos:closeMatch`** (fuzzy matches) — links two concepts "sufficiently similar that they can be used interchangeably in some information retrieval applications" but stops short of asserting full equivalence (Miles & Bechhofer, 2009, §10).
   331→
   332→**Why not `owl:sameAs`:** Halpin et al. show that `owl:sameAs` is routinely misused in linked data to express approximate or cross-type identity, producing unwanted OWL entailments downstream. Their recommendation: reserve `owl:sameAs` for verified same-individual identity; use SKOS mapping properties for concept-level matching (Halpin, Hayes, McCusker, McGuinness & Thompson, *When owl:sameAs Isn't the Same: An Analysis of Identity in Linked Data*, ISWC 2010, DOI: 10.1007/978-3-642-17746-0_16).
   333→
   334→**Match type taxonomy** (exact / normalized / fuzzy) follows the standard candidate generation ladder in entity linking (Shen, Wang & Han, *Entity Linking with a Knowledge Base: Issues, Techniques, and Solutions*, IEEE TKDE 27(2), 2015, §3.1, DOI: 10.1109/TKDE.2014.2327028).
   335→
   336→**Levenshtein distance** (Levenshtein, *Binary Codes Capable of Correcting Deletions, Insertions, and Reversals*, Soviet Physics Doklady 10(8), 1966). The threshold of ≤ 2 is a common empirical choice; validate against the gold set planned in `gnd-title-extraction.md` §Paper §4 quality metrics.
   337→
   338→---
   339→
   340→## Step 4 — Changes to `link_gnd_works.py`
   341→
   342→Replace the lobid-gnd REST call (Steps 3–4 in `gnd-title-extraction.md`) with:
   343→
   344→```python
   345→import httpx
   346→import asyncio
   347→
   348→QLEVER_ENDPOINT = "https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd"
   349→CONCURRENCY = 10  # tune up after rate-limit testing
   350→
   351→_semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(CONCURRENCY)
   352→
   353→async def query_gnd_werk(
   354→    extracted_title: str,
   355→    author_gnd_uri: str | None
   356→) -> list[dict]:
   357→    tokens = tokenize_for_qlever(extracted_title)
   358→    sparql = (
   359→        QUERY_A.format(title_tokens=tokens, author_gnd_uri=author_gnd_uri)
   360→        if author_gnd_uri
   361→        else QUERY_B.format(title_tokens=tokens)
   362→    )
   363→    async with _semaphore:
   364→        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:
   365→            r = await client.post(
   366→                QLEVER_ENDPOINT,
   367→                data={"query": sparql},
   368→                headers={"Accept": "application/sparql-results+json"}
   369→            )
   370→    r.raise_for_status()
   371→    return r.json()["results"]["bindings"]
   372→```
   373→
   374→Pattern selection:
   375→- Pattern A if `author_gnd_uri` is set
   376→- Pattern B otherwise
   377→- Pattern C as fallback if recon 0.2 shows `contains-word` is unavailable
   378→
   379→---
   380→
   381→## Step 5 — What this replaces
   382→
   383→| Old plan | New plan |
   384→|---|---|
   385→| `GET /gnd/search?q=label:"{title}"&filter=type:Work` (lobid REST) | SPARQL POST to QLever endpoint |
   386→| Endpoint TBD / local infrastructure required | Public DNB endpoint, no setup |
   387→| No text relevance ranking | QLever `score()` ranks candidates |
   388→| `link_gnd_works.py` depended on lobid API availability | Depends on `sparql.dnb.de` uptime (DNB-operated) |
   389→| Title-only or title + author_uri via query param | Same semantics; `gndo:firstAuthor` predicate (verify in recon 0.3) |
   390→
   391→---
   392→
   393→## Open questions
   394→
   395→- [ ] **Is `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` in the text index?** Run recon 0.2. Determines Pattern A/B vs Pattern C.
   396→- [ ] **Exact predicate for author link** — `gndo:firstAuthor` or `gndo:creator`? Run recon 0.3.
   397→- [ ] **Umlaut handling in `contains-word`** — does the QLever text index at DNB normalize Umlauts, or must queries use diacritic-stripped tokens? Test `"goethe"` vs `"göthe"` against a known record.
   398→- [ ] **Rate limits on the public endpoint** — no published SLA; start at `CONCURRENCY = 10`, measure latency, tune up cautiously.
   399→



```

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/gnd-linking-spec.md",
  "content": "# GeMeA — GND Werk Linking: Requirements Specification\n\n**Document type:** Software Requirements Specification\n**Framework:** IREB CPRE (Certified Professional for Requirements Engineering)\n**Subject:** `scripts/link_gnd_works.py` — offline GND Werk entity linking\n**Source:** `notes/gnd-linking-plan.md`\n**Status:** Draft\n\n---\n\n## 1. System Context\n\n### 1.1 Purpose\n\n`link_gnd_works.py` enriches Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek (DDB) ProvidedCHO records with GND Werk URIs by matching extracted `dc:title` strings against GND authority records via the DNB SPARQL endpoint. The resulting triples feed the mocho grouping pipeline, which uses the GND Werk URI as the key to cluster ProvidedCHOs into `mocho:Work` entities.\n\n### 1.2 Pipeline position\n\n```\nDDB JSON-LD\n    ↓ rdf2jsonld\nRDF/JSON\n    ↓ link_gnd_works.py        ← this system\nRDF/JSON + GND Werk triples\n    ↓ mocho\nNormalized RDF\n    ↓ QLever + Elasticsearch\nGeMeA API → /work/{id}\n```\n\n`link_gnd_works.py` is an **offline ingest step**. It runs once per re-ingest during Phase 0 and never serves live requests. Throughput and batch efficiency are the governing design constraints; per-query latency is not.\n\n### 1.3 System boundary\n\n| Inside scope | Outside scope |\n|---|---|\n| Title extraction from `dc:title` strings | mocho Work grouping logic |\n| SPARQL queries against `sparql.dnb.de` | GND Werk URI dereferencing |\n| Post-retrieval scoring and match type assignment | ES index build |\n| Triple generation (`skos:exactMatch`, `skos:closeMatch`) | API serving |\n| Deduplication of title/author pairs | GND agent linking (Phase 1b) |\n\n### 1.4 Knowledge base\n\n**Endpoint:** `https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd`\n**Description:** DNB Bibliography and GND (`authorities-gnd_lds`, `dnb-all_lds` v23.02.2026) — updated monthly\n**Scale:** 1,242,168,767 triples; 186,556,644 subjects; 591 predicates\n**Protocol:** SPARQL 1.1 over HTTP POST (`application/x-www-form-urlencoded`); QLever-specific extensions (`contains-word`, `score()`) **not available** on this endpoint (confirmed by recon query 0.2)\n\n---\n\n## 2. Stakeholders\n\n| Stakeholder | Role | Goal |\n|---|---|---|\n| GeMeA development team | Builder + operator | Pipeline produces ≥70% Werk linking rate; triples are correct and loadable into QLever |\n| ISWC 2026 paper reviewers | Evaluators | Quality metrics (precision, coverage) are reproducible and reported transparently |\n| DDB / DNB | Data provider | Public endpoint is not abused; queries stay within acceptable load |\n| End users (GeMeA web UI) | Consumers | `/work/{id}` pages exist for major works; WEMI grouping is meaningful |\n\n---\n\n## 3. Functional Requirements\n\n### FR-01 — Title extraction\n\n**ID:** FR-01\n**Priority:** Must\n**Statement:** The system shall extract a clean title string from each ProvidedCHO `dc:title` value prior to GND lookup.\n**Method:** Two-stage extraction:\n1. Rule-based ISBD parser (primary): split on ` / `, `. - `, ` : ` per ISBD punctuation conventions\n2. NER fallback (secondary): NuNER Zero zero-shot NER on records without ISBD markers (~71.6% of corpus)\n\n**Rationale:** Raw `dc:title` strings contain ISBD-encoded publication metadata (edition, publisher, place, year) concatenated with the title. Sending raw strings to GND produces low-precision matches.\n\n---\n\n### FR-02 — GND Werk lookup\n\n**ID:** FR-02\n**Priority:** Must\n**Statement:** The system shall query the DNB SPARQL endpoint to retrieve GND Werk candidates for each extracted title.\n**Query target classes:** `gndo:Work`, `gndo:MusicalWork`, `gndo:Manuscript` — the three GND subclasses that correspond to IFLA LRM-E2 Work (confirmed by recon query 0.1; combined population: 580,284 entities).\n**Excluded classes:** `gndo:Expression`, `gndo:VersionOfAMusicalWork` (IFLA LRM-E3), `gndo:ProvenanceCharacteristic` (no WEMI equivalent), `gndo:CollectiveManuscript` (zero entities in dataset).\n\n---\n\n### FR-03 — Author-constrained query (Pattern C with author)\n\n**ID:** FR-03\n**Priority:** Must\n**Statement:** When a ProvidedCHO is linked to a person with a GND URI, the system shall constrain the SPARQL query to Works associated with that person.\n**Author predicates:** `gndo:author`, `gndo:firstAuthor`, `gndo:poet`, `gndo:composer` — enumerated explicitly via `VALUES ?authorPred` because SPARQL 1.1 does not infer `rdfs:subPropertyOf` without a reasoner, and `gndo:firstAuthor` is a subproperty of `gndo:author`.\n**Query:**\n```sparql\nPREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>\nSELECT ?work ?prefLabel WHERE {\n  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }\n  VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }\n  ?work a ?wtype .\n  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .\n  ?work ?authorPred <{author_gnd_uri}> .\n  FILTER(LCASE(STR(?prefLabel)) = \"{normalized_title}\")\n}\nLIMIT 10\n```\n\n---\n\n### FR-04 — Title-only query (Pattern C without author)\n\n**ID:** FR-04\n**Priority:** Must\n**Statement:** When no author GND URI is available, the system shall query by normalized title string alone.\n**Query:**\n```sparql\nPREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>\nSELECT ?work ?prefLabel WHERE {\n  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }\n  ?work a ?wtype .\n  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .\n  FILTER(LCASE(STR(?prefLabel)) = \"{normalized_title}\")\n}\nLIMIT 10\n```\n**Note:** QLever `contains-word` text search is not available on this endpoint (confirmed recon 0.2). Pattern C (FILTER on normalized string) is the sole active query path.\n\n---\n\n### FR-05 — Title normalization pipeline\n\n**ID:** FR-05\n**Priority:** Must\n**Statement:** The system shall normalize extracted titles before query construction through three ordered phases:\n\n| Phase | Step | Description |\n|---|---|---|\n| Normalization | Unicode NFC | Canonical Decomposition + Composition (Unicode Annex #15); ensures consistent code-point representation |\n| Normalization | Lowercase | Case-fold after NFC |\n| Normalization | Strip diacritics | NFD decompose → remove combining characters (`unicodedata.category == 'Mn'`); maps `ä→a`, `ü→u`, `ö→o` |\n| Tokenization | Split | Split on whitespace and punctuation into word tokens |\n| Filtering | Remove stopwords | `der, die, das, ein, eine, von, und, zu, im, in, an, auf, für, mit, bei, dem, den` |\n| Filtering | Select distinctive tokens | 2–3 tokens by semantic centrality and low collision frequency (see `gnd-linking-plan.md` §Step 2) |\n\n---\n\n### FR-06 — Post-retrieval match scoring\n\n**ID:** FR-06\n**Priority:** Must\n**Statement:** The system shall assign a match type and confidence score to each candidate returned by the SPARQL query.\n\n| Condition | `match_type` | RDF predicate |\n|---|---|---|\n| `prefLabel == extracted_title` (case-insensitive) | `exact` | `skos:exactMatch` |\n| Normalized match (diacritics + punctuation stripped) | `normalized` | `skos:exactMatch` |\n| Levenshtein distance ≤ 2 to `prefLabel` or any `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` | `fuzzy` | `skos:closeMatch` |\n| No candidate above threshold | `unresolved` | — (no triple written) |\n\n`match_confidence` = normalized edit similarity to `prefLabel` (float 0–1).\n**Predicate rationale:** `skos:exactMatch` (not `owl:sameAs`) is used because a DDB ProvidedCHO and a GND Werk URI are not the same OWL individual; `skos:exactMatch` asserts retrieval-level equivalence without triggering OWL identity entailments (Halpin et al., ISWC 2010).\n\n---\n\n### FR-07 — Triple output\n\n**ID:** FR-07\n**Priority:** Must\n**Statement:** The system shall write one JSON record per ProvidedCHO to `data/raw/gnd-works/`, containing:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"cho_uri\": \"https://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/ABC123\",\n  \"raw_title\": \"...\",\n  \"extracted_title\": \"Faust\",\n  \"extraction_method\": \"isbd\" | \"ner\",\n  \"author_gnd_uri\": \"https://d-nb.info/gnd/118540238\",\n  \"gnd_werk_uri\": \"https://d-nb.info/gnd/118607359\",\n  \"match_type\": \"exact\" | \"normalized\" | \"fuzzy\",\n  \"match_confidence\": 1.0\n}\n```\n\nUnresolved records omit `gnd_werk_uri`, `match_type`, `match_confidence`.\n\n---\n\n### FR-08 — Deduplication\n\n**ID:** FR-08\n**Priority:** Must\n**Statement:** The system shall deduplicate `(extracted_title, author_gnd_uri)` pairs before issuing SPARQL queries and join results back to all ProvidedCHOs sharing the same pair.\n**Rationale:** ~65M ProvidedCHOs share ~5–10M unique title/author pairs. Without deduplication, the same query is issued tens of times for the same pair (e.g., all copies of *Faust* across institutions), wasting endpoint capacity.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Non-Functional Requirements\n\n### NFR-01 — Throughput\n\n**ID:** NFR-01\n**Priority:** Must\n**Statement:** The system shall complete a full run over ~5–10M unique title/author pairs within a time frame compatible with a monthly re-ingest cycle.\n**Initial concurrency:** 10 parallel HTTP requests (`asyncio.Semaphore(10)`); tunable upward after rate-limit profiling of the DNB endpoint.\n\n---\n\n### NFR-02 — Linking coverage\n\n**ID:** NFR-02\n**Priority:** Must\n**Statement:** The system shall achieve ≥70% GND Werk linking rate across all ProvidedCHOs. This is the minimum threshold for enabling the `/work/{id}` API endpoint and WEMI hierarchy pages in GeMeA.\n\n---\n\n### NFR-03 — Linking precision\n\n**ID:** NFR-03\n**Priority:** Must\n**Statement:** Manual spot-check of 100 randomly sampled linked pairs shall show ≥90% correct Werk assignments for `exact` and `normalized` matches.\n\n---\n\n### NFR-04 — Endpoint availability dependency\n\n**ID:** NFR-04\n**Priority:** Should\n**Statement:** The system shall handle transient `sparql.dnb.de` failures gracefully — retry with exponential backoff (max 3 retries), log failures, and continue processing remaining pairs. Failed pairs shall be written to a retry queue for re-processing.\n**Rationale:** The DNB endpoint has no published SLA. A single timeout must not abort a multi-hour batch run.\n\n---\n\n### NFR-05 — Reproducibility\n\n**ID:** NFR-05\n**Priority:** Must\n**Statement:** Given the same input data and the same GND dataset version, the system shall produce identical output. The GND dataset version (`dnb-all_lds` v23.02.2026) shall be recorded in output metadata.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Constraints\n\n### CON-01 — SPARQL 1.1 only\n\n**ID:** CON-01\n**Statement:** The DNB endpoint (`sparql.dnb.de`) enforces SPARQL 1.1 grammar. QLever-specific keywords (`contains-word`, `score()`, `TEXTLIMIT`) are not available and must not be used in production queries.\n**Source:** Confirmed by recon query 0.2 — `contains-word` returns a parse error.\n\n---\n\n### CON-02 — Public endpoint, no SLA\n\n**ID:** CON-02\n**Statement:** `sparql.dnb.de` is a publicly operated endpoint with no published rate limits or uptime SLA. The system must not exceed concurrency levels that could constitute abusive load. Initial limit: 10 concurrent requests.\n\n---\n\n### CON-03 — GND ontology version\n\n**ID:** CON-03\n**Statement:** Query design is based on `gnd_20251218.ttl`. If the GND ontology is updated, author predicates (`gndo:author`, `gndo:firstAuthor`, `gndo:poet`, `gndo:composer`) and Work-class membership must be re-verified.\n\n---\n\n### CON-04 — Offline execution only\n\n**ID:** CON-04\n**Statement:** `link_gnd_works.py` must not be invoked at API request time. It is a batch ingest script. Results are pre-computed and loaded into QLever; the GeMeA API reads from QLever at runtime.\n\n---\n\n### CON-05 — RDF predicate semantics\n\n**ID:** CON-05\n**Statement:** The system shall use `skos:exactMatch` for exact and normalized matches, and `skos:closeMatch` for fuzzy matches. `owl:sameAs` must not be used, as a DDB ProvidedCHO and a GND Werk URI are not the same OWL individual.\n**Reference:** Miles & Bechhofer, SKOS Reference, W3C 2009, §10; Halpin et al., ISWC 2010.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Acceptance Criteria\n\n| ID | Criterion | Verification method |\n|---|---|---|\n| AC-01 | All SPARQL queries conform to SPARQL 1.1 grammar | Run each query template against the endpoint with a known title; no parse errors |\n| AC-02 | `VALUES ?wtype` contains exactly `gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript` | Code review; verified against recon 0.1 results |\n| AC-03 | `VALUES ?authorPred` contains `gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer` | Code review; verified against recon 0.3 results |\n| AC-04 | Levenshtein threshold of ≤ 2 validated against gold set | Spot-check 500 stratified records; adjust if precision < 90% |\n| AC-05 | Deduplication reduces query count from ~65M to ~5–10M | Log unique pair count before and after dedup |\n| AC-06 | ≥70% Werk linking rate on full DDB corpus | Pipeline run report |\n| AC-07 | Output JSON conforms to FR-07 schema | Schema validation on sample output |\n| AC-08 | `owl:sameAs` does not appear in any output triple | `grep owl:sameAs` on output files |\n\n---\n\n## 7. Open Questions\n\n| ID | Question | Blocking |\n|---|---|---|\n| OQ-01 | Does the DNB endpoint normalize Umlauts in FILTER comparisons? (`ä` vs `a`) — affects diacritic stripping in FR-05 | No — test empirically |\n| OQ-02 | What are the actual rate limits on `sparql.dnb.de`? | No — profile during first batch run |\n| OQ-03 | Should `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` be queried in a separate UNION pattern to increase recall for fuzzy matching? Currently only `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` is queried | No |\n| OQ-04 | Does `gndo:composer`/`gndo:firstComposer` behave analogously to `gndo:author`/`gndo:firstAuthor` for MusicalWork records? | No — verify with a musician query analogous to recon 0.3 |\n"
}
```

Created [gnd-linking-spec.md](gemea/notes/gnd-linking-spec.md). Structure follows IREB CPRE conventions:

- **§1 System context** — purpose, pipeline position, boundary table, KB stats
- **§2 Stakeholders** — four stakeholders with goals
- **§3 Functional requirements** (FR-01–08) — title extraction, SPARQL queries (both patterns), normalization pipeline, scoring, triple output, deduplication; each with ID, priority, statement, and rationale
- **§4 Non-functional requirements** (NFR-01–05) — throughput, linking coverage (≥70%), precision (≥90%), availability handling, reproducibility
- **§5 Constraints** (CON-01–05) — SPARQL 1.1 only, no SLA, ontology version dependency, offline-only, predicate semantics
- **§6 Acceptance criteria** — 8 verifiable criteria, each with a concrete verification method
- **§7 Open questions** — 4 items flagged as non-blocking

also write an architecture decision document based on gnd-linking-plan.md

### Assistant *(2026-03-20T23:57:39.614Z)*

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/gnd-linking-adr.md",
  "content": "# GeMeA — Architecture Decision Record: GND Werk Entity Linking\n\n**Format:** MADR (Markdown Architecture Decision Record)\n**Subject:** Design decisions for `scripts/link_gnd_works.py`\n**Source:** `notes/gnd-linking-plan.md`\n**Status:** Accepted\n\n---\n\n## ADR-01 — Use the DNB public SPARQL endpoint instead of a local GND instance\n\n**Status:** Accepted\n**Date:** 2026-03-21\n\n### Context\n\nThe original plan assumed a locally operated GND instance (lobid-gnd REST API) for Werk lookups. The endpoint was listed as TBD, requiring infrastructure setup, data ingestion, and maintenance. The DNB operates a public QLever-backed SPARQL endpoint at `https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd` containing the full GND dataset (1.24B triples, updated monthly).\n\n### Decision\n\nUse `https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd` as the GND lookup endpoint. No local GND infrastructure is required.\n\n### Consequences\n\n**Positive:**\n- No infrastructure setup, ingestion pipeline, or maintenance for a local GND instance\n- Dataset is DNB-authoritative and updated monthly\n- Covers the full GND (580,284 Work-class entities confirmed by recon)\n\n**Negative:**\n- No published SLA; endpoint availability is outside our control\n- No published rate limits; concurrency must be profiled conservatively\n- Cannot add custom indexes or text search configuration\n\n**Mitigations:**\n- Retry with exponential backoff on transient failures (NFR-04)\n- Initial concurrency cap of 10 parallel requests, tunable after profiling\n\n---\n\n## ADR-02 — Use SPARQL 1.1 FILTER instead of QLever `contains-word` for title matching\n\n**Status:** Accepted (forced by recon result)\n**Date:** 2026-03-21\n\n### Context\n\nThe linking plan originally designed three query patterns:\n- **Pattern A** — title + author, using QLever `contains-word` for full-text search with relevance ranking (`score()`)\n- **Pattern B** — title only, using `contains-word` with UNION over `gndo:variantNameForTheWork`\n- **Pattern C** — FILTER fallback for when `contains-word` is unavailable\n\nRecon query 0.2 returned a SPARQL parse error when `contains-word` was used:\n```\nInvalid SPARQL query: Token \"contains-word\": mismatched input 'contains-word'\nexpecting {'(', 'a', '^', '!', IRI_REF, PNAME_NS, PNAME_LN, VAR1, VAR2, PREFIX_LANGTAG}\n```\n\nThe DNB endpoint enforces SPARQL 1.1 grammar. QLever-specific syntax is not available.\n\n### Decision\n\nPattern C (`FILTER(LCASE(STR(?prefLabel)) = \"{normalized_title}\")`) is the sole active query path. Patterns A and B are retained in the plan as reference but are not used.\n\nPattern C is parameterised by author availability:\n- **With author GND URI:** add `VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }` and `?work ?authorPred <{author_gnd_uri}>`\n- **Without author GND URI:** title FILTER only\n\n### Consequences\n\n**Positive:**\n- Fully SPARQL 1.1 compliant; no dependency on QLever extensions\n- Exact and normalized matches are deterministic and reproducible\n\n**Negative:**\n- No relevance ranking — all matches are binary (match / no match); scoring is done entirely in Python post-retrieval\n- No variant name search in a single query — `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` requires a separate query or UNION\n- Fuzzy matching (Levenshtein ≤ 2) must be handled entirely in Python; the SPARQL query retrieves only exact/normalized candidates\n\n**Mitigations:**\n- Post-retrieval scoring in Python covers normalized and fuzzy match types (Step 3)\n- A second UNION pattern over `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` can be added to Pattern C without requiring `contains-word`\n\n---\n\n## ADR-03 — Restrict Work class scope to IFLA LRM-E2 equivalents only\n\n**Status:** Accepted\n**Date:** 2026-03-21\n\n### Context\n\nThe GND ontology defines seven subclasses of `gndo:Work`. Not all correspond to the IFLA LRM Work concept (LRM-E2: \"a distinct intellectual or artistic creation\"). Querying all subclasses would return Expression-level and non-WEMI entities as Werk candidates, producing semantically incorrect links.\n\n### Decision\n\nRestrict `VALUES ?wtype` to the three GND classes that map to IFLA LRM-E2:\n\n```sparql\nVALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }\n```\n\n| Class | IFLA LRM | Population | Decision |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `gndo:Work` | LRM-E2 — direct match | 242,333 | Include |\n| `gndo:MusicalWork` | LRM-E2 — musical composition as distinct creative entity | 330,818 | Include |\n| `gndo:Manuscript` | LRM-E2 — unique historical manuscript is the intellectual creation | 7,133 | Include |\n| `gndo:Expression` | LRM-E3 — realization of a Work | 5,766 | Exclude |\n| `gndo:VersionOfAMusicalWork` | LRM-E3 — specific arrangement/version | 2,905 | Exclude |\n| `gndo:ProvenanceCharacteristic` | No WEMI equivalent | 10,080 | Exclude |\n| `gndo:CollectiveManuscript` | LRM-E4 — container Manifestation | 0 | Exclude |\n\n### Consequences\n\n**Positive:**\n- Links are semantically correct at the WEMI Work level\n- Avoids false positives from Expression-level or non-creative entities\n- Covers 580,284 entities — the full Work-level GND population\n\n**Negative:**\n- `gndo:Expression` entities (5,766) are excluded; DDB records that are specifically linked to an Expression (e.g., a translation) will not receive a Werk link via this pipeline\n- Decision is tied to `gnd_20251218.ttl`; must be re-verified if the ontology changes\n\n---\n\n## ADR-04 — Enumerate author predicates explicitly rather than relying on property inference\n\n**Status:** Accepted\n**Date:** 2026-03-21\n\n### Context\n\nThe GND ontology defines a property hierarchy for creator roles. `gndo:firstAuthor` is declared `rdfs:subPropertyOf gndo:author`. Naively querying only `gndo:author` would miss triples stored as `gndo:firstAuthor`. SPARQL 1.1 does not infer `rdfs:subPropertyOf` without an OWL reasoner.\n\nRecon query 0.3 (Works linked to Goethe, `https://d-nb.info/gnd/118540238`) confirmed:\n- `gndo:author` — 8/10 results (dominant predicate)\n- `gndo:poet` — 1/10 (lyrical works)\n- `gndo:relatedPerson` — 1/10 (too broad; excluded)\n\nThe relevant property hierarchy from `gnd_20251218.ttl`:\n\n| Property | Subproperty of | Scope |\n|---|---|---|\n| `gndo:author` | RDA P60434 | Textual works |\n| `gndo:firstAuthor` | `gndo:author` | Single/lead author |\n| `gndo:poet` | RDA P60477 | Words of non-dramatic musical works |\n| `gndo:composer` | RDA P60426 | Musical works |\n| `gndo:firstComposer` | `gndo:composer` | Single/lead composer |\n\n### Decision\n\nUse `VALUES ?authorPred` to enumerate all relevant author predicates explicitly:\n\n```sparql\nVALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }\n```\n\n`gndo:relatedPerson` is excluded as it is not specific to the creator role. `gndo:firstComposer` is included implicitly via `gndo:composer` only if confirmed by a musician-specific recon query (OQ-04).\n\n### Consequences\n\n**Positive:**\n- Correct recall across all creator role predicates without requiring OWL reasoning\n- Explicit enumeration is transparent and easy to audit\n\n**Negative:**\n- New GND role predicates introduced in future ontology versions will not be covered automatically; requires manual update\n\n---\n\n## ADR-05 — Use `skos:exactMatch` and `skos:closeMatch` instead of `owl:sameAs`\n\n**Status:** Accepted\n**Date:** 2026-03-21\n\n### Context\n\nEntity linking pipelines commonly use `owl:sameAs` to assert that two URIs identify the same entity. However, a DDB ProvidedCHO URI and a GND Werk URI are not the same OWL individual: the ProvidedCHO is a catalog record for a physical manifestation; the Werk URI is an authority record for an abstract work. Asserting `owl:sameAs` between them would be ontologically incorrect and would cause unintended OWL entailments in any downstream reasoner.\n\nHalpin et al. (ISWC 2010, *When owl:sameAs Isn't the Same*) document widespread misuse of `owl:sameAs` for approximate and cross-type identity in linked data, and recommend SKOS mapping properties as the correct alternative for concept-level matching.\n\n### Decision\n\n| Match type | Condition | Predicate |\n|---|---|---|\n| `exact` | Case-insensitive string equality | `skos:exactMatch` |\n| `normalized` | Match after diacritic + punctuation stripping | `skos:exactMatch` |\n| `fuzzy` | Levenshtein distance ≤ 2 | `skos:closeMatch` |\n| `unresolved` | No candidate above threshold | — |\n\n`skos:exactMatch` asserts that two concepts \"can be used interchangeably for all retrieval purposes\" (SKOS Reference, W3C 2009, §10) — appropriate for confirmed title-to-Werk matches without OWL identity entailments. `skos:closeMatch` is used for fuzzy matches where interchangeability holds only in some retrieval contexts.\n\n### Consequences\n\n**Positive:**\n- Semantically correct — no false OWL identity assertions\n- `skos:exactMatch` and `skos:closeMatch` are standard SKOS mapping properties, consumable by any SKOS-aware client\n- Match confidence is preserved in the `match_type` field; downstream consumers can filter by confidence level\n\n**Negative:**\n- SPARQL queries that assume `owl:sameAs` for entity resolution will not find these links; consumers must query for SKOS predicates explicitly\n\n---\n\n## ADR-06 — Deduplicate title/author pairs before querying\n\n**Status:** Accepted\n**Date:** 2026-03-21\n\n### Context\n\nThe DDB corpus contains ~65M ProvidedCHOs, but many share identical `dc:title` strings across institutions (multiple copies of the same work). Issuing one SPARQL query per ProvidedCHO would result in the same query being issued dozens or hundreds of times for popular works (e.g., all copies of *Faust* across German libraries).\n\n### Decision\n\nDeduplicate on `(extracted_title, author_gnd_uri)` pairs before querying:\n1. First pass: collect all pairs from rdf2jsonld output\n2. Issue SPARQL queries for unique pairs only (~5–10M estimated)\n3. Second pass: join results back to all ProvidedCHOs sharing the same pair\n\n### Consequences\n\n**Positive:**\n- Reduces SPARQL queries from ~65M to ~5–10M (10–13× reduction)\n- Reduces load on the public DNB endpoint\n- Proportionally reduces total run time\n\n**Negative:**\n- Requires an intermediate data structure (hash map of pair → result) that may be large in memory for the full corpus; may need disk-backed storage (e.g., SQLite) for the full 65M run\n\n---\n\n## ADR-07 — Run as offline batch ingest, not as a runtime API component\n\n**Status:** Accepted\n**Date:** 2026-03-21\n\n### Context\n\nAn alternative design would have the GeMeA API resolve GND Werk links on demand — querying `sparql.dnb.de` at request time when a user views an item page. This would keep linking results always up-to-date with the latest GND data.\n\n### Decision\n\n`link_gnd_works.py` runs offline during Phase 0 ingest. Results are pre-computed, stored as triples, and loaded into QLever. The API reads from QLever at runtime and never calls `sparql.dnb.de` directly.\n\n### Consequences\n\n**Positive:**\n- API latency is unaffected by `sparql.dnb.de` availability or response time\n- Linking results are stable within an ingest cycle; no per-request variability\n- Throughput and batch efficiency are the governing constraints — latency per query is irrelevant\n\n**Negative:**\n- Linking results are only as fresh as the last ingest; GND updates between ingests are not reflected\n- A full re-ingest is required to pick up GND changes (acceptable given the monthly update cadence)\n"
}
```

Created [gnd-linking-adr.md](gemea/notes/gnd-linking-adr.md). Seven decisions in MADR format, each with context, decision, and consequences (positive/negative/mitigations):

| ADR | Decision |
|---|---|
| ADR-01 | DNB public endpoint over local GND instance |
| ADR-02 | SPARQL 1.1 FILTER over QLever `contains-word` (forced by recon) |
| ADR-03 | Restrict Work types to IFLA LRM-E2 equivalents only |
| ADR-04 | Enumerate author predicates explicitly (no property inference) |
| ADR-05 | `skos:exactMatch`/`skos:closeMatch` over `owl:sameAs` |
| ADR-06 | Deduplicate title/author pairs before querying |
| ADR-07 | Offline batch ingest, not runtime API component |

   290→Example: *"Die Leiden des jungen Werthers"* → tokens `leiden werthers`; the proper noun alone (`werthers`) is sufficient with Pattern A.
   291→
   292→**Criterion 2 — Low collision frequency.** Prefer tokens that appear in few GND Works. Some words are not stopwords but are so common in bibliographic titles that they add almost no discriminating power:
   293→
   294→```python
   295→GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS = {
   296→    "briefe", "gedichte", "werke", "schriften", "geschichte",
   297→    "bericht", "katalog", "sammlung", "aufsätze", "texte",
   298→    "band", "teil", "heft", "nummer", "jahrgang",
   299→}
   300→```
   301→
   302→Example: *"Gedichte"* alone returns thousands of GND Works. *"faust"* alone is sufficient with an author URI.
   303→
   304→This list should be derived empirically from a frequency query against the endpoint, not hardcoded blindly — run after recon 0.1.
   305→
   306→**Decision rule:**
   307→
   308→```python
   309→def select_tokens(tokens: list[str]) -> list[str]:
   310→    # tokens: already stopword-filtered, lowercased, diacritics-stripped
   311→    distinctive = [t for t in tokens if t not in GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS and len(t) > 3]
   312→    return distinctive[:3] if len(distinctive) >= 2 else tokens[:3]
   313→```
   314→

### Assistant *(2026-03-21T00:08:33.842Z)*

Added to Step 2 Criterion 2 in [gnd-linking-plan.md](gemea/notes/gnd-linking-plan.md):

- **Combined query** — single SPARQL request using subquery UNION, one per word, ordered by count descending
- **Per-word curl template** — fallback if the combined query times out; replace `WORD` with each term
- `aufsätze` queried as `aufs` to catch both diacritic and stripped variants
- **Results table** with a "Keep in list?" column — to be filled after running, so words with low counts can be dropped from `GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS`
