{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/scripts/analyse_title_lengths.py",
  "content": "# Purpose:      Load data/DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl, compute whitespace-token\n#               count per title, extract year from title string where present,\n#               and produce a stacked bar chart of title-length categories\n#               (short ≤5, medium 6–20, long >20 tokens) per year bucket.\n#               A second panel shows median token count per year bucket.\n# Usage:        python scripts/analyse_title_lengths.py\n#               python scripts/analyse_title_lengths.py --data PATH --output-dir PATH\n# Inputs:       data/DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl — DataFrame with obj_id + title columns\n# Outputs:      output/fig_title_lengths.png      — stacked bar + median line chart\n#               output/title-length-analysis.json — raw and bucketed counts\n# Dependencies: pandas, matplotlib\n# Assumptions:  DataFrame has 'obj_id' and 'title' columns (confirmed by rate_isbd_fields.py).\n#               Year is extracted from the title string (no dedicated year column).\n\nimport re\nimport json\nimport argparse\nimport pickle\nimport math\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport matplotlib\nmatplotlib.use(\"Agg\")\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\nimport matplotlib.ticker as mticker\nimport pandas as pd\n\nROOT        = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nDATA_PATH   = ROOT / \"data\" / \"DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl\"\nOUTPUT_DIR  = ROOT / \"output\"\n\n# Length category boundaries (token count, whitespace-split)\nSHORT_MAX  = 5    # ≤ 5 tokens\nMEDIUM_MAX = 20   # 6–20 tokens\n# >20 = long\n\n# Year regex: 1400–2029 — same range as rate_isbd_fields.py\nYEAR_RE = re.compile(r\"\\b(?:1[4-9]\\d{2}|20[012]\\d)\\b\")\n\nMAX_BINS = 40  # maximum non-empty year bins before choosing a wider bucket\n\n\n# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ndef extract_year(title: str) -> int | None:\n    \"\"\"Return last 4-digit year found in title (publication year is usually trailing).\"\"\"\n    m = list(YEAR_RE.finditer(title))\n    return int(m[-1].group()) if m else None\n\n\ndef token_count(title: str) -> int:\n    return len(title.split())\n\n\ndef length_category(n: int) -> str:\n    if n <= SHORT_MAX:\n        return \"short\"\n    if n <= MEDIUM_MAX:\n        return \"medium\"\n    return \"long\"\n\n\ndef bucket_counts(year_data: dict[int, list[int]], size: int, year_min: int, year_max: int):\n    \"\"\"\n    year_data: year → list of token counts\n    Returns: dict of bucket_label → {\"short\": n, \"medium\": n, \"long\": n, \"tokens\": [flat list]}\n    \"\"\"\n    start = (year_min // size) * size\n    end   = ((year_max // size) + 1) * size\n    bins  = {}\n    for b in range(start, end, size):\n        label = f\"{b}–{b + size - 1}\"\n        tokens = []\n        for y in range(b, b + size):\n            tokens.extend(year_data.get(y, []))\n        bins[label] = {\n            \"short\":  sum(1 for t in tokens if t <= SHORT_MAX),\n            \"medium\": sum(1 for t in tokens if SHORT_MAX < t <= MEDIUM_MAX),\n            \"long\":   sum(1 for t in tokens if t > MEDIUM_MAX),\n            \"tokens\": tokens,\n        }\n    return bins\n\n\ndef choose_bucket(year_min: int, year_max: int, year_data: dict) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Return smallest bucket size yielding ≤ MAX_BINS non-empty bins.\"\"\"\n    for size in (5, 10, 25, 50, 100):\n        start = (year_min // size) * size\n        end   = ((year_max // size) + 1) * size\n        non_empty = 0\n        for b in range(start, end, size):\n            if any(year_data.get(y) for y in range(b, b + size)):\n                non_empty += 1\n        if non_empty <= MAX_BINS:\n            return size\n    return 100\n\n\n# ── load & compute ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ndef main(data_path: Path, output_dir: Path) -> None:\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    print(f\"Loading {data_path} ...\")\n    with open(data_path, \"rb\") as f:\n        df = pickle.load(f)\n    print(f\"Shape: {df.shape}  |  Columns: {list(df.columns)}\")\n\n    titles = df[\"title\"].dropna().astype(str)\n    total  = len(titles)\n\n    # Per-record token count and year extraction\n    year_data: dict[int, list[int]] = defaultdict(list)  # year → [token_counts]\n    no_year_tokens: list[int] = []\n    all_tokens: list[int] = []\n\n    for title in titles:\n        n    = token_count(title)\n        year = extract_year(title)\n        all_tokens.append(n)\n        if year:\n            year_data[year].append(n)\n        else:\n            no_year_tokens.append(n)\n\n    has_year = sum(len(v) for v in year_data.values())\n    year_min = min(year_data)\n    year_max = max(year_data)\n\n    # Overall length distribution\n    short_n  = sum(1 for t in all_tokens if t <= SHORT_MAX)\n    medium_n = sum(1 for t in all_tokens if SHORT_MAX < t <= MEDIUM_MAX)\n    long_n   = sum(1 for t in all_tokens if t > MEDIUM_MAX)\n    median_overall = sorted(all_tokens)[len(all_tokens) // 2]\n\n    print(f\"\\nTotal titles     : {total:,}\")\n    print(f\"Has year         : {has_year:,}  ({100*has_year/total:.1f}%)\")\n    print(f\"No year          : {len(no_year_tokens):,}  ({100*len(no_year_tokens)/total:.1f}%)\")\n    print(f\"Year range       : {year_min}–{year_max}\")\n    print(f\"\\nOverall length distribution (whitespace tokens):\")\n    print(f\"  Short  (≤{SHORT_MAX:2d})  : {short_n:>7,}  ({100*short_n/total:.1f}%)\")\n    print(f\"  Medium ({SHORT_MAX+1:2d}–{MEDIUM_MAX}) : {medium_n:>7,}  ({100*medium_n/total:.1f}%)\")\n    print(f\"  Long   (>{MEDIUM_MAX:2d})  : {long_n:>7,}  ({100*long_n/total:.1f}%)\")\n    print(f\"  Median token count: {median_overall}\")\n\n    # Year bucketing\n    size = choose_bucket(year_min, year_max, year_data)\n    bins = bucket_counts(year_data, size, year_min, year_max)\n\n    # Drop empty bins; restrict to 1500+ for readable chart\n    non_empty = [\n        (label, data) for label, data in bins.items()\n        if (data[\"short\"] + data[\"medium\"] + data[\"long\"]) > 0\n        and int(label.split(\"–\")[0]) >= 1500\n    ]\n    n_pre1500 = sum(\n        d[\"short\"] + d[\"medium\"] + d[\"long\"]\n        for lbl, d in bins.items()\n        if (d[\"short\"] + d[\"medium\"] + d[\"long\"]) > 0\n        and int(lbl.split(\"–\")[0]) < 1500\n    )\n    print(f\"\\nSelected bucket size: {size} years  ({len(non_empty)} non-empty bins from 1500+)\")\n    print(f\"  Pre-1500 records omitted from chart: {n_pre1500:,}\")\n\n    # Summary table per bucket\n    print(f\"\\n{'Year bucket':<14}  {'Total':>7}  {'Short%':>7}  {'Med%':>7}  {'Long%':>7}  {'Median':>7}\")\n    print(\"-\" * 60)\n    for label, data in non_empty:\n        n = data[\"short\"] + data[\"medium\"] + data[\"long\"]\n        toks = data[\"tokens\"]\n        med = sorted(toks)[len(toks) // 2] if toks else 0\n        s_pct = 100 * data[\"short\"]  / n if n else 0\n        m_pct = 100 * data[\"medium\"] / n if n else 0\n        l_pct = 100 * data[\"long\"]   / n if n else 0\n        print(f\"{label:<14}  {n:>7,}  {s_pct:>6.1f}%  {m_pct:>6.1f}%  {l_pct:>6.1f}%  {med:>7}\")\n\n    # ── save JSON ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    out_json = output_dir / \"title-length-analysis.json\"\n    json_data = {\n        \"total_titles\": total,\n        \"has_year\": has_year,\n        \"no_year\": len(no_year_tokens),\n        \"year_range\": [year_min, year_max],\n        \"bucket_size\": size,\n        \"length_thresholds\": {\"short_max\": SHORT_MAX, \"medium_max\": MEDIUM_MAX},\n        \"overall\": {\n            \"short\": short_n, \"medium\": medium_n, \"long\": long_n,\n            \"median_tokens\": median_overall,\n        },\n        \"bucketed\": {\n            label: {\n                \"short\":  d[\"short\"],\n                \"medium\": d[\"medium\"],\n                \"long\":   d[\"long\"],\n                \"total\":  d[\"short\"] + d[\"medium\"] + d[\"long\"],\n                \"median_tokens\": sorted(d[\"tokens\"])[len(d[\"tokens\"]) // 2] if d[\"tokens\"] else 0,\n            }\n            for label, d in non_empty\n        },\n    }\n    with open(out_json, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(json_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n    print(f\"\\nSaved JSON  : {out_json}\")\n\n    # ── plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    plt.rcParams.update({\n        \"font.family\": \"sans-serif\",\n        \"axes.spines.top\":   False,\n        \"axes.spines.right\": False,\n        \"figure.dpi\": 150,\n    })\n\n    labels     = [lbl for lbl, _ in non_empty]\n    short_vals = [d[\"short\"]  for _, d in non_empty]\n    med_vals   = [d[\"medium\"] for _, d in non_empty]\n    long_vals  = [d[\"long\"]   for _, d in non_empty]\n    totals     = [s + m + l for s, m, l in zip(short_vals, med_vals, long_vals)]\n    medians    = [\n        sorted(d[\"tokens\"])[len(d[\"tokens\"]) // 2] if d[\"tokens\"] else 0\n        for _, d in non_empty\n    ]\n    x = range(len(labels))\n\n    fig_w = max(12, len(labels) * 0.65)\n    fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(\n        2, 1, figsize=(fig_w, 9),\n        gridspec_kw={\"height_ratios\": [3, 1]},\n        sharex=True,\n    )\n\n    # Top panel: stacked bars (absolute counts)\n    COLOR_SHORT  = \"#4C72B0\"   # blue\n    COLOR_MEDIUM = \"#DD8452\"   # orange\n    COLOR_LONG   = \"#55A868\"   # green\n\n    b_short  = ax1.bar(x, short_vals, color=COLOR_SHORT,  width=0.78, linewidth=0, label=f\"Short (≤{SHORT_MAX} tokens)\")\n    b_medium = ax1.bar(x, med_vals,   color=COLOR_MEDIUM, width=0.78, linewidth=0,\n                       bottom=short_vals, label=f\"Medium ({SHORT_MAX+1}–{MEDIUM_MAX} tokens)\")\n    b_long   = ax1.bar(x, long_vals,  color=COLOR_LONG,   width=0.78, linewidth=0,\n                       bottom=[s + m for s, m in zip(short_vals, med_vals)],\n                       label=f\"Long (>{MEDIUM_MAX} tokens)\")\n\n    ax1.set_ylabel(\"Number of titles\", fontsize=10)\n    ax1.set_title(\n        f\"DF_DE_TITLES — Title Length Distribution by Year  ·  {size}-year buckets  ·  N={sum(totals):,}\",\n        fontsize=12, fontweight=\"bold\", pad=10,\n    )\n    ax1.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f\"{int(v):,}\"))\n    ax1.grid(axis=\"y\", alpha=0.3)\n    ax1.legend(frameon=False, fontsize=9, loc=\"upper left\")\n\n    # Annotate total count on each bar\n    vmax = max(totals) if totals else 1\n    for i, tot in enumerate(totals):\n        ax1.text(i, tot + vmax * 0.01, f\"{tot:,}\",\n                 ha=\"center\", va=\"bottom\", fontsize=5.5, rotation=90, color=\"#333333\")\n\n    # Bottom panel: median token count per bucket\n    ax2.plot(x, medians, color=\"#C44E52\", marker=\"o\", linewidth=1.5, markersize=4)\n    ax2.fill_between(x, medians, alpha=0.12, color=\"#C44E52\")\n    ax2.set_ylabel(\"Median\\ntokens\", fontsize=9)\n    ax2.set_xticks(list(x))\n    ax2.set_xticklabels([lbl.split(\"–\")[0] for lbl in labels],\n                        rotation=60, ha=\"right\", fontsize=8)\n    ax2.grid(axis=\"y\", alpha=0.3)\n    ax2.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f\"{int(v)}\"))\n\n    fig.tight_layout()\n    out_png = output_dir / \"fig_title_lengths.png\"\n    fig.savefig(out_png, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n    plt.close(fig)\n    print(f\"Saved chart : {out_png}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Analyse title-length distribution in DF_DE_TITLES\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--data\",       type=Path, default=DATA_PATH)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--output-dir\", type=Path, default=OUTPUT_DIR)\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n    main(args.data, args.output_dir)\n"
}
```

short (≤5) rises to 8–14% from 1775 onward.\n- The overall corpus median of 7 tokens is driven by the post-1750 majority.\n- 85.3% of titles contain no extractable year — the per-year table covers only the minority with an explicit year in the title string and may not be representative of the full corpus across eras.\n- **Implication for SR-07 (gold set):** stratify by length (short / medium / long) as well as era; pre-1750 records are systematically longer and will stress the NER model differently from modern titles."

     1→# scripts/

     3→Standalone operational scripts for GeMeA. Each script is self-contained with a header comment.
     4→
     5→## Conventions
     6→
     7→Every script must begin with:
     8→```
     9→# Purpose:      one-line description
    10→# Usage:        how to invoke
    11→# Inputs:       what it reads
    12→# Outputs:      what it writes
    13→# Dependencies: external tools / packages required
    14→# Assumptions:  preconditions

    18→
    19→## Index
    20→
    21→| Script | Purpose |
    22→|--------|---------|
    23→| `download_data.sh` | Fetch mocho-normalized RDF from public data dump |
    24→| `setup_gnd_fuseki.sh` | (deprecated) Start local Fuseki container and load GND authority files |
    25→| `setup_gnd_qlever.sh` | Convert GND JSON-LD to N-Triples, build QLever index, start SPARQL server |
    26→| `jsonld_to_nt.py` | Convert a JSON-LD file (.jsonld or .jsonld.gz) to N-Triples using rdflib |
    27→| `check_generic_title_words.py` | Query DNB SPARQL endpoint to count GND Work records per GENERIC_TITLE_WORD; validates which words are high-collision and should be excluded from distinctive token selection |
    28→| `check_isbd_titles.py` | Analyse ISBD punctuation patterns in DF_DE_TITLES; reports per-pattern counts and sample titles |
    29→| `rate_isbd_fields.py` | Rate all 4.47M DF_DE_TITLES records for the presence of bibliographic fields (TITLE, PERSON, PUBLISHER, PLACE, YEAR, EDITION, SERIES, VOLUME) using ISBD rules; assigns silver_tier for NER training data selection; optionally writes per-pattern examples with DDB links |
    30→| `validate_heuristic_fields.py` | Sample 200 heuristic-tier records from isbd_field_ratings.csv for manual false-positive review; writes a review sheet CSV with DDB links and blank fp_fields / notes columns |
    31→

### Assistant *(2026-03-23T14:31:01.700Z)*

Done. Summary of what was produced:

**Script** — [analyse_title_lengths.py](gemea/scripts/analyse_title_lengths.py): loads the pkl, computes whitespace token counts, extracts years from title strings, auto-selects 25-year buckets (same logic as `analyse_years.py`), produces a stacked bar chart + median line panel.

**Key findings written into SR-10:**
- Overall: 41% short (≤5 tokens), 49% medium, 10% long; median 7 tokens
- Pre-1750 titles are dramatically longer (median 31–43 tokens, 62–76% long) — early modern verbose ISBD-style descriptions
- Post-1775 titles are shorter (median 8–13 tokens), consistent with modern cataloging
- 85.3% of titles have no year in the string, so the per-year table covers only the year-bearing minority
- **Implication for SR-07**: gold set must stratify by length as well as era; pre-1750 records stress the NER model differently

```
     1→# Purpose:      Load data/DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl, compute whitespace-token
     2→#               count per title, extract year from title string where present,
     3→#               and produce a stacked bar chart of title-length categories
     4→#               (short ≤5, medium 6–20, long >20 tokens) per year bucket.
     5→#               A second panel shows median token count per year bucket.
     6→# Usage:        python scripts/analyse_title_lengths.py
     7→#               python scripts/analyse_title_lengths.py --data PATH --output-dir PATH
     8→# Inputs:       data/DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl — DataFrame with obj_id + title columns
     9→# Outputs:      output/fig_title_lengths.png      — stacked bar + median line chart
    10→#               output/title-length-analysis.json — raw and bucketed counts
    11→# Dependencies: pandas, matplotlib
    12→# Assumptions:  DataFrame has 'obj_id' and 'title' columns (confirmed by rate_isbd_fields.py).
    13→#               Year is extracted from the title string (no dedicated year column).
    14→
    15→import re
    16→import json
    17→import argparse
    18→import pickle
    19→import math
    20→from collections import defaultdict
    21→from pathlib import Path
    22→
    23→import matplotlib
    24→matplotlib.use("Agg")
    25→import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    26→import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
    27→import pandas as pd
    28→
    29→ROOT        = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
    30→DATA_PATH   = ROOT / "data" / "DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl"
    31→OUTPUT_DIR  = ROOT / "output"
    32→
    33→# Length category boundaries (token count, whitespace-split)
    34→SHORT_MAX  = 5    # ≤ 5 tokens
    35→MEDIUM_MAX = 20   # 6–20 tokens
    36→# >20 = long
    37→
    38→# Year regex: 1400–2029 — same range as rate_isbd_fields.py
    39→YEAR_RE = re.compile(r"\b(?:1[4-9]\d{2}|20[012]\d)\b")
    40→
    41→MAX_BINS = 40  # maximum non-empty year bins before choosing a wider bucket
    42→
    43→
    44→# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    45→
    46→def extract_year(title: str):
    47→    """Return last 4-digit year found in title (publication year is usually trailing)."""
    48→    m = list(YEAR_RE.finditer(title))
    49→    return int(m[-1].group()) if m else None
    50→
    51→
    52→def token_count(title: str) -> int:
    53→    return len(title.split())
    54→
    55→
    56→def length_category(n: int) -> str:
    57→    if n <= SHORT_MAX:
    58→        return "short"
    59→    if n <= MEDIUM_MAX:
    60→        return "medium"
    61→    return "long"
    62→
    63→
    64→def bucket_counts(year_data: dict[int, list[int]], size: int, year_min: int, year_max: int):
    65→    """
    66→    year_data: year → list of token counts
    67→    Returns: dict of bucket_label → {"short": n, "medium": n, "long": n, "tokens": [flat list]}
    68→    """
    69→    start = (year_min // size) * size
    70→    end   = ((year_max // size) + 1) * size
    71→    bins  = {}
    72→    for b in range(start, end, size):
    73→        label = f"{b}–{b + size - 1}"
    74→        tokens = []
    75→        for y in range(b, b + size):
    76→            tokens.extend(year_data.get(y, []))
    77→        bins[label] = {
    78→            "short":  sum(1 for t in tokens if t <= SHORT_MAX),
    79→            "medium": sum(1 for t in tokens if SHORT_MAX < t <= MEDIUM_MAX),
    80→            "long":   sum(1 for t in tokens if t > MEDIUM_MAX),
    81→            "tokens": tokens,
    82→        }
    83→    return bins
    84→
    85→
    86→def choose_bucket(year_min: int, year_max: int, year_data: dict) -> int:
    87→    """Return smallest bucket size yielding ≤ MAX_BINS non-empty bins."""
    88→    for size in (5, 10, 25, 50, 100):
    89→        start = (year_min // size) * size
    90→        end   = ((year_max // size) + 1) * size
    91→        non_empty = 0
    92→        for b in range(start, end, size):
    93→            if any(year_data.get(y) for y in range(b, b + size)):
    94→                non_empty += 1
    95→        if non_empty <= MAX_BINS:
    96→            return size
    97→    return 100
    98→
    99→
   100→# ── load & compute ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   101→
   102→def main(data_path: Path, output_dir: Path) -> None:
   103→    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   104→
   105→    print(f"Loading {data_path} ...")
   106→    with open(data_path, "rb") as f:
   107→        df = pickle.load(f)
   108→    print(f"Shape: {df.shape}  |  Columns: {list(df.columns)}")
   109→
   110→    titles = df["title"].dropna().astype(str)
   111→    total  = len(titles)
   112→
   113→    # Per-record token count and year extraction
   114→    year_data: dict[int, list[int]] = defaultdict(list)  # year → [token_counts]
   115→    no_year_tokens: list[int] = []
   116→    all_tokens: list[int] = []
   117→
   118→    for title in titles:
   119→        n    = token_count(title)
   120→        year = extract_year(title)
   121→        all_tokens.append(n)
   122→        if year:
   123→            year_data[year].append(n)
   124→        else:
   125→            no_year_tokens.append(n)
   126→
   127→    has_year = sum(len(v) for v in year_data.values())
   128→    year_min = min(year_data)
   129→    year_max = max(year_data)
   130→
   131→    # Overall length distribution
   132→    short_n  = sum(1 for t in all_tokens if t <= SHORT_MAX)
   133→    medium_n = sum(1 for t in all_tokens if SHORT_MAX < t <= MEDIUM_MAX)
   134→    long_n   = sum(1 for t in all_tokens if t > MEDIUM_MAX)
   135→    median_overall = sorted(all_tokens)[len(all_tokens) // 2]
   136→
   137→    print(f"\nTotal titles     : {total:,}")
   138→    print(f"Has year         : {has_year:,}  ({100*has_year/total:.1f}%)")
   139→    print(f"No year          : {len(no_year_tokens):,}  ({100*len(no_year_tokens)/total:.1f}%)")
   140→    print(f"Year range       : {year_min}–{year_max}")
   141→    print(f"\nOverall length distribution (whitespace tokens):")
   142→    print(f"  Short  (≤{SHORT_MAX:2d})  : {short_n:>7,}  ({100*short_n/total:.1f}%)")
   143→    print(f"  Medium ({SHORT_MAX+1:2d}–{MEDIUM_MAX}) : {medium_n:>7,}  ({100*medium_n/total:.1f}%)")
   144→    print(f"  Long   (>{MEDIUM_MAX:2d})  : {long_n:>7,}  ({100*long_n/total:.1f}%)")
   145→    print(f"  Median token count: {median_overall}")
   146→
   147→    # Year bucketing
   148→    size = choose_bucket(year_min, year_max, year_data)
   149→    bins = bucket_counts(year_data, size, year_min, year_max)
   150→
   151→    # Drop empty bins; restrict to 1500+ for readable chart
   152→    non_empty = [
   153→        (label, data) for label, data in bins.items()
   154→        if (data["short"] + data["medium"] + data["long"]) > 0
   155→        and int(label.split("–")[0]) >= 1500
   156→    ]
   157→    n_pre1500 = sum(
   158→        d["short"] + d["medium"] + d["long"]
   159→        for lbl, d in bins.items()
   160→        if (d["short"] + d["medium"] + d["long"]) > 0
   161→        and int(lbl.split("–")[0]) < 1500
   162→    )
   163→    print(f"\nSelected bucket size: {size} years  ({len(non_empty)} non-empty bins from 1500+)")
   164→    print(f"  Pre-1500 records omitted from chart: {n_pre1500:,}")
   165→
   166→    # Summary table per bucket
   167→    print(f"\n{'Year bucket':<14}  {'Total':>7}  {'Short%':>7}  {'Med%':>7}  {'Long%':>7}  {'Median':>7}")
   168→    print("-" * 60)
   169→    for label, data in non_empty:
   170→        n = data["short"] + data["medium"] + data["long"]
   171→        toks = data["tokens"]
   172→        med = sorted(toks)[len(toks) // 2] if toks else 0
   173→        s_pct = 100 * data["short"]  / n if n else 0
   174→        m_pct = 100 * data["medium"] / n if n else 0
   175→        l_pct = 100 * data["long"]   / n if n else 0
   176→        print(f"{label:<14}  {n:>7,}  {s_pct:>6.1f}%  {m_pct:>6.1f}%  {l_pct:>6.1f}%  {med:>7}")
   177→
   178→    # ── save JSON ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   179→
   180→    out_json = output_dir / "title-length-analysis.json"
   181→    json_data = {
   182→        "total_titles": total,
   183→        "has_year": has_year,
   184→        "no_year": len(no_year_tokens),
   185→        "year_range": [year_min, year_max],
   186→        "bucket_size": size,
   187→        "length_thresholds": {"short_max": SHORT_MAX, "medium_max": MEDIUM_MAX},
   188→        "overall": {
   189→            "short": short_n, "medium": medium_n, "long": long_n,
   190→            "median_tokens": median_overall,
   191→        },
   192→        "bucketed": {
   193→            label: {
   194→                "short":  d["short"],
   195→                "medium": d["medium"],
   196→                "long":   d["long"],
   197→                "total":  d["short"] + d["medium"] + d["long"],
   198→                "median_tokens": sorted(d["tokens"])[len(d["tokens"]) // 2] if d["tokens"] else 0,
   199→            }
   200→            for label, d in non_empty
   201→        },
   202→    }
   203→    with open(out_json, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   204→        json.dump(json_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
   205→    print(f"\nSaved JSON  : {out_json}")
   206→
   207→    # ── plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   208→
   209→    plt.rcParams.update({
   210→        "font.family": "sans-serif",
   211→        "axes.spines.top":   False,
   212→        "axes.spines.right": False,
   213→        "figure.dpi": 150,
   214→    })
   215→
   216→    labels     = [lbl for lbl, _ in non_empty]
   217→    short_vals = [d["short"]  for _, d in non_empty]
   218→    med_vals   = [d["medium"] for _, d in non_empty]
   219→    long_vals  = [d["long"]   for _, d in non_empty]
   220→    totals     = [s + m + l for s, m, l in zip(short_vals, med_vals, long_vals)]
   221→    medians    = [
   222→        sorted(d["tokens"])[len(d["tokens"]) // 2] if d["tokens"] else 0
   223→        for _, d in non_empty
   224→    ]
   225→    x = range(len(labels))
   226→
   227→    fig_w = max(12, len(labels) * 0.65)
   228→    fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(
   229→        2, 1, figsize=(fig_w, 9),
   230→        gridspec_kw={"height_ratios": [3, 1]},
   231→        sharex=True,
   232→    )
   233→
   234→    # Top panel: stacked bars (absolute counts)
   235→    COLOR_SHORT  = "#4C72B0"   # blue
   236→    COLOR_MEDIUM = "#DD8452"   # orange
   237→    COLOR_LONG   = "#55A868"   # green
   238→
   239→    b_short  = ax1.bar(x, short_vals, color=COLOR_SHORT,  width=0.78, linewidth=0, label=f"Short (≤{SHORT_MAX} tokens)")
   240→    b_medium = ax1.bar(x, med_vals,   color=COLOR_MEDIUM, width=0.78, linewidth=0,
   241→                       bottom=short_vals, label=f"Medium ({SHORT_MAX+1}–{MEDIUM_MAX} tokens)")
   242→    b_long   = ax1.bar(x, long_vals,  color=COLOR_LONG,   width=0.78, linewidth=0,
   243→                       bottom=[s + m for s, m in zip(short_vals, med_vals)],
   244→                       label=f"Long (>{MEDIUM_MAX} tokens)")
   245→
   246→    ax1.set_ylabel("Number of titles", fontsize=10)
   247→    ax1.set_title(
   248→        f"DF_DE_TITLES — Title Length Distribution by Year  ·  {size}-year buckets  ·  N={sum(totals):,}",
   249→        fontsize=12, fontweight="bold", pad=10,
   250→    )
   251→    ax1.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f"{int(v):,}"))
   252→    ax1.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.3)
   253→    ax1.legend(frameon=False, fontsize=9, loc="upper left")
   254→
   255→    # Annotate total count on each bar
   256→    vmax = max(totals) if totals else 1
   257→    for i, tot in enumerate(totals):
   258→        ax1.text(i, tot + vmax * 0.01, f"{tot:,}",
   259→                 ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=5.5, rotation=90, color="#333333")
   260→
   261→    # Bottom panel: median token count per bucket
   262→    ax2.plot(x, medians, color="#C44E52", marker="o", linewidth=1.5, markersize=4)
   263→    ax2.fill_between(x, medians, alpha=0.12, color="#C44E52")
   264→    ax2.set_ylabel("Median\ntokens", fontsize=9)
   265→    ax2.set_xticks(list(x))
   266→    ax2.set_xticklabels([lbl.split("–")[0] for lbl in labels],
   267→                        rotation=60, ha="right", fontsize=8)
   268→    ax2.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.3)
   269→    ax2.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f"{int(v)}"))
   270→
   271→    fig.tight_layout()
   272→    out_png = output_dir / "fig_title_lengths.png"
   273→    fig.savefig(out_png, bbox_inches="tight")
   274→    plt.close(fig)
   275→    print(f"Saved chart : {out_png}")
   276→
   277→
   278→if __name__ == "__main__":
   279→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Analyse title-length distribution in DF_DE_TITLES")
   280→    parser.add_argument("--data",       type=Path, default=DATA_PATH)
   281→    parser.add_argument("--output-dir", type=Path, default=OUTPUT_DIR)
   282→    args = parser.parse_args()
   283→    main(args.data, args.output_dir)
   284→

  "content": "# Purpose:      Load data/DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl and plot title-length\n#               distribution per year bucket. Length is taken from the\n#               pre-computed 'all_tokens' and 'content_tokens' columns;\n#               both are shown together on the median panel. Year is taken\n#               from the 'dates' column; titles with no 'dates' value fall\n#               back to a regex extraction from the title string.\n# Usage:        python scripts/analyse_title_lengths.py\n#               python scripts/analyse_title_lengths.py --data PATH --output-dir PATH\n# Inputs:       data/DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl — DataFrame with obj_id, title,\n#                 all_tokens, content_tokens, dates columns\n# Outputs:      output/fig_title_lengths.png      — stacked bar + dual median chart\n#               output/title-length-analysis.json — bucketed counts and medians\n# Dependencies: pandas, matplotlib\n# Assumptions:  'dates' column is a string year (e.g. '1931') or NaN.\n#               'all_tokens' and 'content_tokens' are int64 token counts.\n\nimport re\nimport json\nimport argparse\nimport pickle\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport matplotlib\nmatplotlib.use(\"Agg\")\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\nimport matplotlib.ticker as mticker\nimport pandas as pd\n\nROOT       = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nDATA_PATH  = ROOT / \"data\" / \"DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl\"\nOUTPUT_DIR = ROOT / \"output\"\n\nSHORT_MAX  = 5   # ≤ 5 tokens  → short\nMEDIUM_MAX = 20  # 6–20 tokens → medium  |  >20 → long\n\n# Fallback: extract year from title string (1400–2029)\nYEAR_RE = re.compile(r\"\\b(?:1[4-9]\\d{2}|20[012]\\d)\\b\")\n\nMAX_BINS = 40  # widest bucket before stepping up\n\n\n# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ndef year_from_title(title: str):\n    \"\"\"Last 4-digit year found in the title string, or None.\"\"\"\n    m = list(YEAR_RE.finditer(str(title)))\n    return int(m[-1].group()) if m else None\n\n\ndef bucket_data(year_map, size, year_min, year_max):\n    \"\"\"\n    year_map: year (int) → {\"all\": [int, ...], \"content\": [int, ...]}\n    Returns ordered list of (label, dict) with short/medium/long counts\n    and median lists for both token types.\n    \"\"\"\n    start = (year_min // size) * size\n    end   = ((year_max // size) + 1) * size\n    bins  = {}\n    for b in range(start, end, size):\n        label = f\"{b}–{b + size - 1}\"\n        all_t, con_t = [], []\n        for y in range(b, b + size):\n            if y in year_map:\n                all_t.extend(year_map[y][\"all\"])\n                con_t.extend(year_map[y][\"content\"])\n        bins[label] = {\n            \"short\":   sum(1 for t in all_t if t <= SHORT_MAX),\n            \"medium\":  sum(1 for t in all_t if SHORT_MAX < t <= MEDIUM_MAX),\n            \"long\":    sum(1 for t in all_t if t > MEDIUM_MAX),\n            \"all_t\":   all_t,\n            \"con_t\":   con_t,\n        }\n    return bins\n\n\ndef choose_bucket(year_min, year_max, year_map):\n    for size in (5, 10, 25, 50, 100):\n        start = (year_min // size) * size\n        end   = ((year_max // size) + 1) * size\n        non_empty = sum(\n            1 for b in range(start, end, size)\n            if any(y in year_map for y in range(b, b + size))\n        )\n        if non_empty <= MAX_BINS:\n            return size\n    return 100\n\n\ndef median(lst):\n    s = sorted(lst)\n    return s[len(s) // 2] if s else 0\n\n\n# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ndef main(data_path: Path, output_dir: Path) -> None:\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    print(f\"Loading {data_path} ...\")\n    with open(data_path, \"rb\") as f:\n        df = pickle.load(f)\n    total = len(df)\n    print(f\"Shape: {df.shape}\")\n\n    # ── year resolution ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    # Primary: 'dates' column (string year or NaN)\n    # Fallback: regex on title string\n\n    dates_null   = df[\"dates\"].isna().sum()\n    dates_source = 0   # resolved from 'dates'\n    title_source = 0   # resolved from title fallback\n    no_year      = 0\n\n    year_map = defaultdict(lambda: {\"all\": [], \"content\": []})\n\n    for _, row in df.iterrows():\n        all_t = int(row[\"all_tokens\"])\n        con_t = int(row[\"content_tokens\"])\n\n        year = None\n        if pd.notna(row[\"dates\"]) and df[\"content_tokens\"].tolist()\n\n    short_n  = sum(1 for t in all_all if t <= SHORT_MAX)\n    medium_n = sum(1 for t in all_all if SHORT_MAX < t <= MEDIUM_MAX)\n    long_n   = sum(1 for t in all_all if t > MEDIUM_MAX)\n\n    print(f\"\\nTotal titles        : {total:,}\")\n    print(f\"Year from 'dates'   : {dates_source:,}  ({100*dates_source/total:.1f}%)\")\n    print(f\"Year from title     : {title_source:,}  ({100*title_source/total:.1f}%)\")\n    print(f\"No year             : {no_year:,}  ({100*no_year/total:.1f}%)\")\n    print(f\"Year range          : {year_min}–{year_max}\")\n    print(f\"\\nOverall (all_tokens):\")\n    print(f\"  Short  (≤{SHORT_MAX})   : {short_n:>7,}  ({100*short_n/total:.1f}%)\")\n    print(f\"  Medium ({SHORT_MAX+1}–{MEDIUM_MAX}) : {medium_n:>7,}  ({100*medium_n/total:.1f}%)\")\n    print(f\"  Long   (>{MEDIUM_MAX})  : {long_n:>7,}  ({100*long_n/total:.1f}%)\")\n    print(f\"  Median all_tokens     : {median(all_all)}\")\n    print(f\"  Median content_tokens : {median(all_con)}\")\n\n    # ── bucketing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    size = choose_bucket(year_min, year_max, year_map)\n    bins = bucket_data(year_map, size, year_min, year_max)\n\n    non_empty = [\n        (lbl, d) for lbl, d in bins.items()\n        if (d[\"short\"] + d[\"medium\"] + d[\"long\"]) > 0\n        and int(lbl.split(\"–\")[0]) >= 1500\n    ]\n    n_pre1500 = sum(\n        d[\"short\"] + d[\"medium\"] + d[\"long\"]\n        for lbl, d in bins.items()\n        if (d[\"short\"] + d[\"medium\"] + d[\"long\"]) > 0\n        and int(lbl.split(\"–\")[0]) < 1500\n    )\n    print(f\"\\nBucket size: {size} years  ({len(non_empty)} non-empty bins from 1500+)\")\n    print(f\"Pre-1500 records omitted from chart: {n_pre1500:,}\")\n\n    print(f\"\\n{'Year bucket':<14}  {'Total':>7}  {'Short%':>7}  {'Med%':>6}  {'Long%':>6}  \"\n          f\"{'Med all_t':>9}  {'Med con_t':>9}\")\n    print(\"-\" * 72)\n    for lbl, d in non_empty:\n        n   = d[\"short\"] + d[\"medium\"] + d[\"long\"]\n        s_p = 100 * d[\"short\"]  / n if n else 0\n        m_p = 100 * d[\"medium\"] / n if n else 0\n        l_p = 100 * d[\"long\"]   / n if n else 0\n        print(f\"{lbl:<14}  {n:>7,}  {s_p:>6.1f}%  {m_p:>5.1f}%  {l_p:>5.1f}%  \"\n              f\"{median(d['all_t']):>9}  {median(d['con_t']):>9}\")\n\n    # ── save JSON ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    out_json = output_dir / \"title-length-analysis.json\"\n    with open(out_json, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump({\n            \"total_titles\": total,\n            \"year_source\": {\n                \"dates_column\": dates_source,\n                \"title_fallback\": title_source,\n                \"no_year\": no_year,\n            },\n            \"year_range\": [year_min, year_max],\n            \"bucket_size\": size,\n            \"length_thresholds\": {\"short_max\": SHORT_MAX, \"medium_max\": MEDIUM_MAX},\n            \"overall\": {\n                \"short\": short_n, \"medium\": medium_n, \"long\": long_n,\n                \"median_all_tokens\": median(all_all),\n                \"median_content_tokens\": median(all_con),\n            },\n            \"bucketed\": {\n                lbl: {\n                    \"short\": d[\"short\"], \"medium\": d[\"medium\"], \"long\": d[\"long\"],\n                    \"total\": d[\"short\"] + d[\"medium\"] + d[\"long\"],\n                    \"median_all_tokens\":     median(d[\"all_t\"]),\n                    \"median_content_tokens\": median(d[\"con_t\"]),\n                }\n                for lbl, d in non_empty\n            },\n        }, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n    print(f\"\\nSaved JSON  : {out_json}\")\n\n    # ── plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    plt.rcParams.update({\n        \"font.family\": \"sans-serif\",\n        \"axes.spines.top\": False,\n        \"axes.spines.right\": False,\n        \"figure.dpi\": 150,\n    })\n\n    x          = list(range(len(non_empty)))\n    xlabels    = [lbl.split(\"–\")[0] for lbl, _ in non_empty]\n    short_vals = [d[\"short\"]  for _, d in non_empty]\n    med_vals   = [d[\"medium\"] for _, d in non_empty]\n    long_vals  = [d[\"long\"]   for _, d in non_empty]\n    totals     = [s + m + l for s, m, l in zip(short_vals, med_vals, long_vals)]\n    med_all    = [median(d[\"all_t\"])  for _, d in non_empty]\n    med_con    = [median(d[\"con_t\"])  for _, d in non_empty]\n\n    COLOR_SHORT  = \"#4C72B0\"\n    COLOR_MEDIUM = \"#DD8452\"\n    COLOR_LONG   = \"#55A868\"\n    COLOR_ALL    = \"#C44E52\"\n    COLOR_CON    = \"#8172B3\"\n\n    fig_w = max(12, len(x) * 0.65)\n    fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(\n        2, 1, figsize=(fig_w, 9),\n        gridspec_kw={\"height_ratios\": [3, 1]},\n        sharex=True,\n    )\n\n    # Top: stacked bar by all_tokens length category\n    ax1.bar(x, short_vals, color=COLOR_SHORT,  width=0.78, linewidth=0,\n            label=f\"Short (≤{SHORT_MAX} tokens)\")\n    ax1.bar(x, med_vals,   color=COLOR_MEDIUM, width=0.78, linewidth=0,\n            bottom=short_vals,\n            label=f\"Medium ({SHORT_MAX+1}–{MEDIUM_MAX} tokens)\")\n    ax1.bar(x, long_vals,  color=COLOR_LONG,   width=0.78, linewidth=0,\n            bottom=[s + m for s, m in zip(short_vals, med_vals)],\n            label=f\"Long (>{MEDIUM_MAX} tokens)\")\n\n    ax1.set_ylabel(\"Number of titles\", fontsize=10)\n    ax1.set_title(\n        f\"DF_DE_TITLES — Title Length by Year  ·  {size}-year buckets  ·  \"\n        f\"N={sum(totals):,} with year  ·  {100*no_year/total:.1f}% no year\",\n        fontsize=11, fontweight=\"bold\", pad=10,\n    )\n    ax1.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f\"{int(v):,}\"))\n    ax1.grid(axis=\"y\", alpha=0.3)\n    ax1.legend(frameon=False, fontsize=9, loc=\"upper left\")\n\n    vmax = max(totals) if totals else 1\n    for i, tot in enumerate(totals):\n        ax1.text(i, tot + vmax * 0.01, f\"{tot:,}\",\n                 ha=\"center\", va=\"bottom\", fontsize=5.5, rotation=90, color=\"#333333\")\n\n    # Bottom: median all_tokens vs median content_tokens\n    ax2.plot(x, med_all, color=COLOR_ALL, marker=\"o\", linewidth=1.5, markersize=4,\n             label=\"median all_tokens\")\n    ax2.plot(x, med_con, color=COLOR_CON, marker=\"s\", linewidth=1.5, markersize=4,\n             linestyle=\"--\", label=\"median content_tokens\")\n    ax2.fill_between(x, med_con, med_all, alpha=0.10, color=\"#888888\",\n                     label=\"stopword overhead\")\n    ax2.set_ylabel(\"Median\\ntokens\", fontsize=9)\n    ax2.set_xticks(x)\n    ax2.set_xticklabels(xlabels, rotation=60, ha=\"right\", fontsize=8)\n    ax2.grid(axis=\"y\", alpha=0.3)\n    ax2.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f\"{int(v)}\"))\n    ax2.legend(frameon=False, fontsize=8, loc=\"upper right\")\n\n    fig.tight_layout()\n    out_png = output_dir / \"fig_title_lengths.png\"\n    fig.savefig(out_png, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n    plt.close(fig)\n    print(f\"Saved chart : {out_png}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n        description=\"Analyse title-length distribution in DF_DE_TITLES\"\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\"--data\",       type=Path, default=DATA_PATH)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--output-dir\", type=Path, default=OUTPUT_DIR)\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n    main(args.data, args.output_dir)\n"
}

1500+):\n\n| Year bucket | Total | Short% | Medium% | Long% | Median all_t | Median con_t |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| 1500–1524 | 12,209 | 20.1% | 48.2% | 31.6% | 13 | 7 |\n| 1525–1549 | 23,901 | 20.2% | 48.8% | 31.0% | 13 | 7 |\n| 1550–1574 | 33,802 | 21.5% | 47.9% | 30.5% | 12 | 6 |\n| 1575–1599 | 37,307 | 24.1% | 43.3% | 32.6% | 12 | 6 |\n| 1600–1624 | 57,887 | 19.0% | 44.1% | 36.8% | 14 | 7 |\n| 1625–1649 | 36,795 | 21.9% | 37.0% | 41.1% | 15 | 8 |\n| 1650–1674 | 56,317 | 21.0% | 39.2% | 39.7% | 14 | 7 |\n| 1675–1699 | 65,723 | 19.9% | 40.7% | 39.4% | 15 | 7 |\n| 1700–1724 | 112,587 | 22.0% | 43.1% | 34.9% | 13 | 6 |\n| 1725–1749 | 125,802 | 22.8% | 45.5% | 31.6% | 12 | 6 |\n| 1750–1774 | 183,051 | 27.0% | 50.2% | 22.8% | 10 | 5 |\n| 1775–1799 | 406,016 | 40.7% | 44.7% | 14.6% | 7 | 4 |\n| 1800–1824 | 195,586 | 29.5% | 49.6% | 20.9% | 9 | 5 |\n| 1825–1849 | 318,929 | 35.5% | 50.2% | 14.3% | 7 | 5 |\n| 1850–1874 | 364,664 | 29.7% | 59.2% | 11.1% | 9 | 5 |\n| 1875–1899 | 503,814 | 44.6% | 48.6% | 6.8% | 6 | 4 |\n| 1900–1924 | 624,305 | 45.4% | 50.3% | 4.3% | 6 | 4 |\n| 1925–1949 | 267,685 | 41.8% | 48.9% | 9.3% | 7 | 4 |\n| 1950–1974 | 107,850 | 42.8% | 48.7% | 8.4% | 7 | 4 |\n| 1975–1999 | 106,457 | 37.7% | 55.8% | 6.5% | 8 | 5 |\n| 2000–2024 | 400,569 | 13.4% | 76.5% | 10.1% | 11 | 6 |\n\n**Key findings:**\n- Pre-1750 titles are systematically long: 31–41% long (>20 tokens), median `all_tokens` 12–15. These are full ISBD-qualified bibliographic strings, not bare titles.\n- Post-1775: sharp shift — median drops to 6–9 tokens; long titles fall to under 15%. Short titles (≤5) rise to 40–45% in the 1875–1949 peak.\n- 2000–2024 reverses the trend: only 13% short, 77% medium — likely digital-born metadata with richer structured descriptions.\n- `content_tokens` (stopwords removed) is consistently ~3 tokens below `all_tokens` median, showing a stable stopword overhead across all eras.\n- **Implication for SR-07 (gold set):** stratify by length as well as era; pre-1750 long-form records stress the NER model differently from the short modern majority. The 9.6% no-year group needs separate treatment — sample by `dc_type` or `silver_tier` instead."
}
```

  "content": "# Purpose:      Plot the raw token-count distribution of all_tokens and\n#               content_tokens in DF_DE_TITLES to identify natural breakpoints\n#               for short/medium/long length categories.\n# Usage:        python scripts/explore_token_distribution.py\n#               python scripts/explore_token_distribution.py --data PATH --output-dir PATH\n# Inputs:       data/DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl — DataFrame with all_tokens, content_tokens\n# Outputs:      output/fig_token_distribution.png — histogram + percentile markers\n#               output/token-distribution.json    — percentile table and value counts\n# Dependencies: pandas, matplotlib, numpy\n# Assumptions:  all_tokens and content_tokens are int64 columns.\n\nimport json\nimport argparse\nimport pickle\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport numpy as np\nimport matplotlib\nmatplotlib.use(\"Agg\")\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\nimport matplotlib.ticker as mticker\n\nROOT       = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nDATA_PATH  = ROOT / \"data\" / \"DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl\"\nOUTPUT_DIR = ROOT / \"output\"\n\nPERCENTILES = [10, 25, 33, 50, 66, 75, 90, 95, 99]\n\n\ndef main(data_path: Path, output_dir: Path) -> None:\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    print(f\"Loading {data_path} ...\")\n    with open(data_path, \"rb\") as f:\n        df = pickle.load(f)\n\n    all_t = df[\"all_tokens\"].values\n    con_t = df[\"content_tokens\"].values\n    total = len(all_t)\n\n    # ── percentile table ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    print(f\"\\nTotal titles: {total:,}\")\n    print(f\"\\n{'Percentile':<12}  {'all_tokens':>12}  {'content_tokens':>14}\")\n    print(\"-\" * 42)\n    pct_all, pct_con = {}, {}\n    for p in PERCENTILES:\n        v_all = int(np.percentile(all_t, p))\n        v_con = int(np.percentile(con_t, p))\n        pct_all[p] = v_all\n        pct_con[p] = v_con\n        print(f\"  p{p:<9}  {v_all:>12}  {v_con:>14}\")\n\n    # ── value counts (all_tokens, first 40) ───────────────────────────────────\n    cap99 = int(np.percentile(all_t, 99))\n    unique, counts = np.unique(all_t, return_counts=True)\n    print(f\"\\nall_tokens value counts (1–40, p99={cap99}):\")\n    print(f\"  {'tokens':>6}  {'count':>8}  {'%':>6}  bar\")\n    print(\"  \" + \"-\" * 55)\n    max_n = counts.max()\n    for v, n in zip(unique, counts):\n        if v > 40:\n            break\n        bar = \"█\" * int(40 * n / max_n)\n        print(f\"  {v:>6}  {n:>8,}  {100*n/total:>5.1f}%  {bar}\")\n\n    # ── save JSON ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    out_json = output_dir / \"token-distribution.json\"\n    with open(out_json, \"w\") as f:\n        json.dump({\n            \"total_titles\": total,\n            \"p99_all_tokens\": cap99,\n            \"percentiles\": {\n                \"all_tokens\":     {str(p): v for p, v in pct_all.items()},\n                \"content_tokens\": {str(p): v for p, v in pct_con.items()},\n            },\n            \"value_counts_all_tokens\": {\n                str(int(v)): int(n) for v, n in zip(unique, counts) if v <= 60\n            },\n        }, f, indent=2)\n    print(f\"\\nSaved JSON  : {out_json}\")\n\n    # ── plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    plt.rcParams.update({\n        \"font.family\": \"sans-serif\",\n        \"axes.spines.top\":   False,\n        \"axes.spines.right\": False,\n        \"figure.dpi\": 150,\n    })\n\n    fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))\n\n    for ax, data, pct_vals, label, color in [\n        (ax1, all_t, pct_all, \"all_tokens\",     \"#4C72B0\"),\n        (ax2, con_t, pct_con, \"content_tokens\", \"#8172B3\"),\n    ]:\n        cap = int(np.percentile(data, 99))\n        vals = data[data <= cap]\n        ax.hist(vals, bins=cap, color=color, alpha=0.8, linewidth=0)\n\n        for p, ls, lw in [(25, \":\", 1.2), (50, \"--\", 1.5), (75, \":\", 1.2), (90, \"-.\", 1.2)]:\n            v = pct_vals[p]\n            ax.axvline(v, color=\"#C44E52\", linewidth=lw, linestyle=ls,\n                       label=f\"p{p} = {v}\")\n\n        ax.set_xlabel(\"Token count\", fontsize=10)\n        ax.set_ylabel(\"Number of titles\", fontsize=10)\n        ax.set_title(f\"{label}  (shown ≤ p99 = {cap})\\nN = {total:,}\",\n                     fontsize=11, fontweight=\"bold\")\n        ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f\"{int(v):,}\"))\n        ax.legend(frameon=False, fontsize=8, loc=\"upper right\")\n        ax.grid(axis=\"y\", alpha=0.3)\n\n    fig.suptitle(\"DF_DE_TITLES — Token count distribution\", fontsize=12,\n                 fontweight=\"bold\", y=1.01)\n    fig.tight_layout()\n    out_png = output_dir / \"fig_token_distribution.png\"\n    fig.savefig(out_png, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n    plt.close(fig)\n    print(f\"Saved chart : {out_png}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n        description=\"Explore token-count distribution in DF_DE_TITLES\"\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\"--data\",       type=Path, default=DATA_PATH)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--output-dir\", type=Path, default=OUTPUT_DIR)\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n    main(args.data, args.output_dir)\n"
}

### Assistant *(2026-03-23T15:20:13.051Z)*

Results are in SR-10. Key findings from the actual distribution:

- The distribution is flat from 1–9 tokens (~5–8% each), then declines — no single obvious cutoff.
- **p25 = 4, p50 = 8, p75 = 14, p90 = 24** are the natural landmarks.
- The previous ≤5/6–20/>20 split was my own suggestion with no empirical basis — the ">20" threshold only catches the top ~13% of titles.
- Three data-driven options are presented in the note; quartile-based (≤4 / 5–14 / >14) is recommended, but the call is yours. Once you confirm the thresholds I'll update `analyse_title_lengths.py`.

### User *(2026-03-23T15:23:11.690Z)*

### Assistant *(2026-03-23T15:23:18.612Z)*

From the value counts already captured:

- **≤ 4**: p25 = 28.3% (by definition)
- **5–14**: p25 to p75 = 50% (by definition — that's what quartiles mean)
- **> 14**: above p75 = 25% (by definition)

But to double-check with exact counts from the value-count output, the p25=4 and p75=14 thresholds split the 4.477M titles into equal 25/50/25 outer groups by construction. The "quartiles" option in the table is exactly that split.

```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/title-length-thresholds.md",
  "content": "# GeMeA — Title Length Category Thresholds\n\n**Applies to:** `scripts/analyse_title_lengths.py`, gold set stratification (SR-07), NER evaluation (SR-08)\n**Source data:** `data/DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl` — 4,477,780 German DDB titles\n**Token count column:** `all_tokens` (pre-computed whitespace-token count)\n\n---\n\n## Decision\n\n| Category | Threshold | Basis |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Short** | ≤ 4 tokens | p25 |\n| **Medium** | 5–14 tokens | p25–p75 |\n| **Long** | > 14 tokens | above p75 |\n\nThresholds are the **quartile boundaries** (p25 = 4, p75 = 14) of the `all_tokens` distribution across the full corpus. This produces equal-sized outer groups (≈25% each) and a 50% middle band, grounded in the data rather than arbitrary round numbers.\n\n---\n\n## Empirical basis\n\nScript: `scripts/explore_token_distribution.py` — output: `output/fig_token_distribution.png`, `output/token-distribution.json`.\n\nPercentile table (`all_tokens`):\n\n| Percentile | all_tokens | content_tokens |\n|---|---|---|\n| p10 | 2 | 1 |\n| p25 | 4 | 2 |\n| p33 | 5 | 3 |\n| p50 | 8 | 5 |\n| p66 | 12 | 6 |\n| p75 | 14 | 8 |\n| p90 | 24 | 13 |\n| p95 | 36 | 19 |\n| p99 | 74 | 40 |\n\nResulting corpus split:\n\n| Category | Count | % |\n|---|---|---|\n| Short (≤ 4) | 1,269,034 | 28.3% |\n| Medium (5–14) | 2,110,610 | 47.1% |\n| Long (> 14) | 1,098,136 | 24.5% |\n| **Total** | **4,477,780** | **100%** |\n\nDistribution shape: roughly flat from 1–9 tokens (5–8% each, peaking at 4 tokens with 8.0%), then steadily declining. Notable bump at 20 tokens (1.9% vs. 1.3% at 19 and 1.0% at 21) — likely a truncation artifact in source data.\n\n---\n\n## Alternatives considered and rejected\n\n| Option | Short | Medium | Long | Reason rejected |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| Arbitrary (original) | ≤ 5 | 6–20 | > 20 | No empirical basis; \"long\" = only top 13% |\n| Terciles | ≤ 5 (p33) | 6–12 | > 12 | \"Long\" = top 34%, too broad |\n| Tail-focused | ≤ 5 (p33) | 6–24 | > 24 | Reserves \"long\" for top 10%; useful for outlier analysis but obscures the gradient |\n| **Quartiles** | ≤ 4 (p25) | 5–14 | > 14 | **Selected** — equal outer groups, natural shoulder at p75 |\n"
}
```

long falls to 12–24%. Short (≤4) rises to 35–38% in the 1875–1949 period.\n- 2000–2024 reverses: only 9% short, 62% medium, 29% long — digital-born metadata with richer structured descriptions.\n- `content_tokens` runs consistently ~3 tokens below `all_tokens` median — stable stopword overhead across all eras.\n- **Implication for SR-07 (gold set):** stratify by length as well as era; pre-1750 long-form records stress the NER model differently from the short modern majority.    - SPARQL 1.1 only (contains-word unavailable); Pattern C (FILTER exact match) is sole active query path
   - ISBD rule-based title extraction + NuNER Zero NER fallback (~71% of records)
   - DF_DE_TITLES: 4,477,780 German DDB titles (dc:language + langid = German), source notebook "2023.11 NER.ipynb"
   - `all_tokens`: whitespace token count including stopwords; `content_tokens`: stopwords removed
   - `dates` column: string year (e.g. '1931'), 89.4% coverage; `date` column: boolean flag only
   - Quartile-based length thresholds: p25=4, p75=14 from the empirical distribution
   - Token distribution: flat 1–9 tokens (5–8% each), peak at 4 tokens (8.0%), notable bump at 20 tokens
   - Pre-1750 titles: 42–50% long (>14 tokens), median 12–15 — verbose ISBD strings
   - Post-1775: shift to shorter titles, median 6–9 tokens
   - IREB CPRE spec + MADR ADR format for GND linking documentation

     # Purpose: Plot title-length distribution per year bucket using pre-computed

     # Year from 'dates' column (clamped 1400–2029), fallback to title regex
     SHORT_MAX  = 4   # ≤4 tokens  → short (p25)
     MEDIUM_MAX = 14  # 5–14 tokens → medium (p25–p75) | >14 → long
     YEAR_RE = re.compile(r"\b(?:1[4-9]\d{2}|20[012]\d)\b")
     MAX_BINS = 40
     # Chart: two panels
     #   Top: stacked bar (short/medium/long counts per year bucket)
     #   Bottom: dual median lines (all_tokens vs content_tokens) with shaded overhead

     1→# GeMeA — NER for Bibliographic Title Extraction
     2→
     3→Context: NER is the **fallback** in `link_gnd_works.py` for records without ISBD markers. The primary extractor is the rule-based ISBD parser. NER only runs when that fails.
     4→
     5→**Scale of the fallback**: analysis of 115K Goethe-Faust DDB items ([isbd-title-analysis.md](../../goethe-faust/notes/isbd-title-analysis.md)) shows only **~29% of titles carry any ISBD pattern**, meaning NER applies to ~71% of records — the majority, not a small edge case. The dominant ISBD signal is ` :` (18%); the ` / ` split used by the parser appears in only 2.1% of titles. These figures are from one DDB provider subset and may vary across the full corpus.
     6→
     7→Target label set: `TITLE`, `OTHER_TITLE`, `PERSON`, `TRANSLATOR`, `PARALLEL_TITLE`, `LANGUAGE`, `MEDIUM`, `EDITION`, `PUBLISHER`, `PLACE`, `YEAR`, `SERIES`, `VOLUME`.
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## 1. Summary
    12→
    13→| Section | What it covers |
    14→|---|---|
    15→| [3. FRBR label scope](#3-frbr-label-scope) | Label definitions organised by FRBR level (Work, Expression, Manifestation) |
    16→| [4. Historical language scope](#4-historical-language-scope) | Risk assessment for pre-modern German and Latin titles; impact on model choice |
    17→| [5. Model options](#5-model-options) | Comparison table: spaCy, Flair, gbert, xlm-roberta, LLM, NuNER Zero, GLiNER, GROBID |
    18→| [6. NuNER Zero — zero-shot NER](#6-nunner-zero--zero-shot-ner-current-recommendation) | Current recommendation: usage code, merge logic, evaluation requirement |
    19→| [7. LLM options at inference time](#7-llm-options-at-inference-time) | Cost/effort comparison for all-NER-record inference paths |
    20→| [8. If no labeled training data is available](#8-if-no-labeled-training-data-is-available) | Silver labeling from ISBD pipeline; LLM one-time labeler; distant supervision |
    21→| [9. Available datasets for fine-tuning](#9-available-datasets-for-fine-tuning) | empathyai/books-ner, HIPE-2022, CLEF-HIPE-2020, GermEval 2014 |
    22→| [10. Decision](#10-decision) | Recommended path: NuNER Zero → LLM labeling → xlm-roberta fine-tune |
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## 2. Open questions
    27→
    28→**SR** = Study/Research question. Numbered in discovery order; not all will appear in the paper.
    29→
    30→| ID | Title | Status | Blocks |
    31→|---|---|---|---|
    32→| [SR-01](#21-sr-01--isbd-signal-coverage-corpus-wide) | ISBD signal coverage (corpus-wide) | ✅ Resolved | — |
    33→| [SR-02](#22-sr-02--isbd-parser-split-priority) | ISBD parser split priority | ✅ Resolved | — |
    34→| [SR-03](#23-sr-03--silver-label-quality-and-false-positive-rate) | Silver label quality and false positive rate | 🔲 Open | [SR-08](#28-sr-08--nunner-zero-evaluation) |
    35→| [SR-04](#24-sr-04--translator--person-disambiguation) | TRANSLATOR / PERSON disambiguation | 🔲 Open | [SR-07](#27-sr-07--gold-set-composition) |
    36→| [SR-05](#25-sr-05--trailing-period-noise) | Trailing period noise | 🔲 Open | — |
    37→| [SR-06](#26-sr-06--historical-and-latin-title-scope) | Historical and Latin title scope | 🔲 Open | [SR-07](#27-sr-07--gold-set-composition) |
    38→| [SR-07](#27-sr-07--gold-set-composition) | Gold set composition | 🔲 Open | [SR-08](#28-sr-08--nunner-zero-evaluation) |
    39→| [SR-08](#28-sr-08--nunner-zero-evaluation) | NuNER Zero evaluation | 🔲 Open — blocked on SR-07 | — |
    40→| [SR-09](#29-sr-09--frbr-metric-scope-for-paper) | FRBR metric scope for paper | 🔲 Open | [SR-07](#27-sr-07--gold-set-composition) |
    41→| [SR-10](#210-sr-10--df_de_titles-source-and-title-length-scope) | DF_DE_TITLES source and title-length scope | ✅ Resolved | — |
    42→
    43→### 2.1 SR-01 — ISBD signal coverage (corpus-wide)
    44→**Status:** Resolved — [isbd-field-rating.md](isbd-field-rating.md)
    45→- DF_DE_TITLES (4.47M): 20.2% have ` :`, 0.8% have ` /`, 14.6% have a year, 3.6% have an edition keyword
    46→- Area separator `. -` present in only **1.2%** of records (53k) — structural tier is limited; heuristic tier carries 99% of silver candidates
    47→- Tier 2 silver (structural, multi-field): **4,613 records** (0.1%)
    48→- Tier 1 silver (heuristic, partial): **335,524 records** (7.5%)
    49→
    50→### 2.2 SR-02 — ISBD parser split priority
    51→**Status:** Resolved
    52→- ` /` (SoR) appears in only 0.8% of titles; ` :` (subtitle) at 20.2% is the dominant title-area signal
    53→- Parser must prioritise ` :` splitting for `OTHER_TITLE` / `TITLE` boundary, not ` /`
    54→
    55→### 2.3 SR-03 — Silver label quality and false positive rate
    56→**Status:** Open — blocked on validation
    57→- Tier-2 labels are structurally derived (`. -` area separator present) — expected high precision
    58→- Tier-1 labels use heuristic whole-string patterns — false positive rate unknown for ` :`, ` /`, YEAR
    59→- **Action:** run `scripts/validate_heuristic_fields.py` (200-record stratified sample); accept tier-1 for augmentation only if false positive rate < 15% per field
    60→
    61→### 2.4 SR-04 — TRANSLATOR / PERSON disambiguation
    62→**Status:** Open
    63→- ` /` fires for both PERSON (author) and TRANSLATOR; keyword matching ("übersetzt von", "Übers.:", "transl.") provides first-pass split
    64→- **Action:** validate keyword heuristic precision on 100-record sample of ` /`-flagged titles before using TRANSLATOR as a distinct silver label
    65→
    66→### 2.5 SR-05 — Trailing period noise
    67→**Status:** Open
    68→- Trailing `.` fires in 17.5% of corpus but also hits abbreviations (`Hrsg.`, `Bd.`) and ordinals — upper bound, not a clean signal
    69→- **Action:** before using as silver label signal, require co-occurrence with another ISBD marker, or strip a curated German abbreviation list
    70→
    71→### 2.6 SR-06 — Historical and Latin title scope
    72→**Status:** Open
    73→- 92.4% of records (tier 0) have no ISBD signals and fall to NER fallback — unknown share are Latin or pre-modern German
    74→- **Action:** sample 200 records from `dc_type` = Leichenpredigt / pre-1800 Monografie; estimate Latin / Early Modern German proportion to determine how much historical signal is needed in training
    75→
    76→### 2.7 SR-07 — Gold set composition
    77→**Status:** Open — blocks SR-08
    78→- **Requirement:** ~500 manually annotated records stratified by: era (modern / 19th c. / pre-1800 / Latin), silver tier (2 / 1 / 0), and `dc_type`
    79→- Must cover tier-0 fallback records (the NER majority path) not just ISBD-structured ones
    80→



```

### User *(2026-03-23T16:10:37.373Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→# Purpose:      Sample 200 heuristic-tier records from isbd_field_ratings.csv and
     3→#               present each title with its detected field flags for manual false-positive
     4→#               review. Writes a review sheet (CSV) pre-filled with detected flags;
     5→#               reviewer adds a 'fp_fields' column listing any fields that are false
     6→#               positives, and 'notes' for comments.
     7→# Usage:        python3 scripts/validate_heuristic_fields.py [--ratings PATH]
     8→#                   [--data PATH] [--output PATH] [--n N] [--seed N]
     9→# Inputs:       data/processed/isbd_field_ratings.csv — output of rate_isbd_fields.py
    10→#               data/DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl        — for DDB URL construction
    11→# Outputs:      data/processed/heuristic_validation_sample.csv — review sheet
    12→# Dependencies: pandas
    13→# Assumptions:  isbd_field_ratings.csv exists (run rate_isbd_fields.py first).
    14→#               Heuristic tier = silver_tier 1 records with has_dot_dash == False.
    15→
    16→import argparse
    17→import pickle
    18→from pathlib import Path
    19→
    20→import pandas as pd
    21→
    22→ROOT            = Path(__file__).parent.parent
    23→RATINGS_DEFAULT = ROOT / "data" / "processed" / "isbd_field_ratings.csv"
    24→DATA_DEFAULT    = ROOT / "data" / "DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl"
    25→OUTPUT_DEFAULT  = ROOT / "data" / "processed" / "heuristic_validation_sample.csv"
    26→DDB_ITEM_URL    = "https://ddb.de/item/{}"
    27→
    28→# Heuristic-only field flags (those that can produce false positives without `. -`)
    29→HEURISTIC_FIELDS = [
    30→    "f_other_title",     # ` :` fires on non-ISBD colons
    31→    "f_person",          # ` /` fires on fractions / paths
    32→    "f_person_compound", # ` / ... ;` — compound SoR check
    33→    "f_parallel",        # ` =` fires on equality signs
    34→    "f_edition",         # edition keywords — generally reliable but worth checking
    35→    "f_year",            # 4-digit year fires on non-publication years
    36→    "f_publisher",       # Verlag/Press keyword — low recall, but check precision
    37→    "f_series",          # parenthetical + digit — generally reliable
    38→    "f_volume",          # volume keyword — generally reliable
    39→]
    40→
    41→
    42→def main() -> None:
    43→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
    44→        description="Sample heuristic-tier records for manual false-positive review."
    45→    )
    46→    parser.add_argument("--ratings", type=Path, default=RATINGS_DEFAULT,
    47→                        help="Path to isbd_field_ratings.csv (default: %(default)s)")
    48→    parser.add_argument("--data",    type=Path, default=DATA_DEFAULT,
    49→                        help="Path to DF_DE_TITLES pkl, used only for dc_type column "
    50→                             "if present (default: %(default)s)")
    51→    parser.add_argument("--output",  type=Path, default=OUTPUT_DEFAULT,
    52→                        help="Path to output review sheet (default: %(default)s)")
    53→    parser.add_argument("--n",       type=int, default=200,
    54→                        help="Number of records to sample (default: %(default)s)")
    55→    parser.add_argument("--seed",    type=int, default=42,
    56→                        help="Random seed for reproducibility (default: %(default)s)")
    57→    args = parser.parse_args()
    58→
    59→    # --- Load ratings ---
    60→    print(f"Loading ratings from {args.ratings} ...")
    61→    ratings = pd.read_csv(args.ratings, low_memory=False)
    62→    print(f"  {len(ratings):,} total records")
    63→
    64→    # --- Select heuristic-tier records (silver_tier >= 1, has_dot_dash == False) ---
    65→    heuristic = ratings[
    66→        (ratings["has_dot_dash"] == False) &
    67→        (ratings["silver_tier"] >= 1)
    68→    ].copy()
    69→    print(f"  {len(heuristic):,} heuristic-tier records (silver_tier≥1, no area separator)")
    70→
    71→    if len(heuristic) == 0:
    72→        raise ValueError("No heuristic-tier records found. Check ratings file.")
    73→
    74→    # --- Stratified sample: one record per detected field flag where possible ---
    75→    # Sample proportionally from each dominant detected field to cover all flag types
    76→    n = min(args.n, len(heuristic))
    77→    per_field = max(1, n // len(HEURISTIC_FIELDS))
    78→    parts = []
    79→    seen_ids = set()
    80→
    81→    for field in HEURISTIC_FIELDS:
    82→        if field not in heuristic.columns:
    83→            continue
    84→        pool = heuristic[(heuristic[field] == 1) & (~heuristic["obj_id"].isin(seen_ids))]
    85→        take = min(per_field, len(pool))
    86→        if take > 0:
    87→            sampled = pool.sample(n=take, random_state=args.seed)
    88→            parts.append(sampled)
    89→            seen_ids.update(sampled["obj_id"].tolist())
    90→
    91→    # Top up to n with any remaining heuristic records not yet selected
    92→    remaining_needed = n - len(seen_ids)
    93→    if remaining_needed > 0:
    94→        leftover = heuristic[~heuristic["obj_id"].isin(seen_ids)]
    95→        if len(leftover) > 0:
    96→            extra = leftover.sample(n=min(remaining_needed, len(leftover)),
    97→                                    random_state=args.seed)
    98→            parts.append(extra)
    99→
   100→    sample = pd.concat(parts, ignore_index=True).head(n)
   101→
   102→    # --- Optionally join dc_type from the PKL for context ---
   103→    if args.data.exists():
   104→        try:
   105→            print(f"Loading dc_type from {args.data} ...")
   106→            with open(args.data, "rb") as f:
   107→                df_full = pickle.load(f)
   108→            if "dc_type" in df_full.columns:
   109→                sample = sample.merge(
   110→                    df_full[["obj_id", "dc_type"]].drop_duplicates("obj_id"),
   111→                    on="obj_id", how="left"
   112→                )
   113→        except Exception as e:
   114→            print(f"  Warning: could not load PKL ({e}); skipping dc_type join")
   115→
   116→    # --- Build review sheet ---
   117→    sample["ddb_url"] = sample["obj_id"].apply(DDB_ITEM_URL.format)
   118→
   119→    # Reviewer columns (blank — to be filled in)
   120→    sample["fp_fields"] = ""   # comma-separated list of field names that are false positives
   121→    sample["notes"]     = ""   # free-text notes
   122→
   123→    # Column order: context first, then flags, then reviewer columns
   124→    flag_cols   = [c for c in HEURISTIC_FIELDS if c in sample.columns]
   125→    meta_cols   = ["obj_id", "ddb_url", "title"]
   126→    if "dc_type" in sample.columns:
   127→        meta_cols.append("dc_type")
   128→    meta_cols  += ["silver_tier", "n_fields", "has_dot_dash"]
   129→    review_cols = ["fp_fields", "notes"]
   130→
   131→    out_cols = meta_cols + flag_cols + review_cols
   132→    out_cols = [c for c in out_cols if c in sample.columns]
   133→    sample = sample[out_cols]
   134→
   135→    # --- Write ---
   136→    args.output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   137→    sample.to_csv(args.output, index=False)
   138→    print(f"\nWrote {len(sample)} records → {args.output}")
   139→
   140→    # --- Summary ---
   141→    print(f"\nField flag counts in sample:")
   142→    print(f"  {'Field':<20}  {'Flagged':>7}  {'%':>6}")
   143→    print(f"  {'-'*38}")
   144→    for field in flag_cols:
   145→        n_flagged = sample[field].sum()
   146→        print(f"  {field:<20}  {n_flagged:>7}  {n_flagged/len(sample)*100:>5.1f}%")
   147→
   148→    print(f"""
   149→Review instructions:
   150→  1. Open {args.output}
   151→  2. For each row, check whether the detected field flags are correct given the title string
   152→  3. In 'fp_fields', list any field names that are FALSE POSITIVES (comma-separated)
   153→     e.g. "f_other_title,f_year"  if both fired incorrectly
   154→  4. Leave 'fp_fields' blank if all detected flags are correct
   155→  5. Use 'notes' for observations (e.g. "year is a page number, not publication year")
   156→""")
   157→
   158→
   159→if __name__ == "__main__":
   160→    main()
   161→



```

    80→
    81→### 2.8 SR-08 — NuNER Zero evaluation
    82→**Status:** Open — blocked on SR-07
    83→- **Requirement:** run NuNER Zero zero-shot on 500 stratified fallback records; assess TITLE and PERSON F1 on gold set
    84→- **Decision gate:** precision ≥ threshold → use zero-shot; else → LLM labeling + fine-tune `xlm-roberta-base` on silver + LLM-labeled set
    85→
    86→### 2.9 SR-09 — FRBR metric scope for paper
    87→**Status:** Open
    88→- **Requirement:** confirm which FRBR levels the paper's quality metrics cover — Work (TITLE, PERSON) only, or also Expression (TRANSLATOR, PARALLEL_TITLE, MEDIUM) and Manifestation (PUBLISHER, PLACE, YEAR, EDITION, SERIES, VOLUME)
    89→- Determines which label types must appear in the gold set and which NER labels are in scope for the evaluation section
    90→
    91→### 2.10 SR-10 — DF_DE_TITLES source and title-length scope
    92→**Status:** Partially resolved — provenance traced; length distribution open
    93→
    94→**Provenance (resolved).** `DF_DE_TITLES` was traced via `grep -rHn "pkl_vars.*DF_DE_TITLES" *.ipynb` across the notebook archive. The variable originates in `2023.11 NER.ipynb` — the earliest notebook in the series and the one that defines the construction. `2024.01 MT-QA.ipynb` produced a separately timestamped pickle (`DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl`) but is not the source. The definition from `2023.11 NER.ipynb`, consistent with `2023.12 Relation Extraction.ipynb`, is:
    95→
    96→> "4,477,641 objects are titles of all TEXT objects, tagged to be in German (`dc:language`) and identified by `langid` to be in German."
    97→
    98→Selection criteria from the `2023.11 NER.ipynb` / `2024.01 MT-QA.ipynb` shared header:
    99→
   100→| | DDB | No. Records |
   101→|---|---|---|
   102→| Total Titles | | 16,805,998 |
   103→| TEXT | | 8,402,999 |
   104→| Languages | | 236 |
   105→| No Language Tags | | 1,521,242 (18.10%) |
   106→| Valid HTYPES (% of TEXT) | | 1,812,559 (21.57%) |
   107→| Languages of Valid HTYPES | | 224 |
   108→| No Language Tags (% of VALID) | | 384,405 (21.21%) |
   109→| **Titles tagged+ident as 'DE'** | | **4,477,641 (53.29%)** |
   110→
   111→`DF_DE_TITLES` is therefore a **language filter** applied to the full DDB TEXT object dump: records where `dc:language` is tagged German AND `langid` confirms German. It is not filtered by `dc:type`, provider, or era. Tokenization in the source notebook uses `spacy.load` (model unspecified in the trace).
   112→
   113→**Implication for generalizability.** The corpus includes both long ISBD strings and short bare-title strings across all eras and `dc:type` values. No length or era filter was applied. ISBD coverage figures (20.2% ` :`, 0.8% ` /`) reflect this broad population.
   114→
   115→**Token-count distribution (resolved).** `scripts/explore_token_distribution.py` — raw distribution of `all_tokens` and `content_tokens` across all 4,477,780 titles. Output: `output/fig_token_distribution.png`, `output/token-distribution.json`.
   116→
   117→Percentile table:
   118→
   119→| Percentile | all_tokens | content_tokens |
   120→|---|---|---|
   121→| p10 | 2 | 1 |
   122→| p25 | 4 | 2 |
   123→| p33 | 5 | 3 |
   124→| p50 | 8 | 5 |
   125→| p66 | 12 | 6 |
   126→| p75 | 14 | 8 |
   127→| p90 | 24 | 13 |
   128→| p95 | 36 | 19 |
   129→| p99 | 74 | 40 |
   130→
   131→Value-count histogram (all_tokens 1–20): the distribution is roughly flat from 1–9 tokens (5–8% each), with a peak at 4 tokens (8.0%), then declines steadily. Notable bump at 20 tokens (1.9% vs. 1.3% at 19 and 1.0% at 21) — likely a truncation artifact in the source data.
   132→
   133→Threshold decision: **quartiles (≤4 / 5–14 / >14)** — p25 = 4, p75 = 14, equal outer groups. See [title-length-thresholds.md](title-length-thresholds.md) for the full empirical basis, alternatives considered, and rejection rationale.
   134→
   135→**Title-length distribution (resolved).** `scripts/analyse_title_lengths.py` — token counts from pre-computed `all_tokens` (includes stopwords) and `content_tokens` (stopwords removed) columns; year from `dates` column (1400–2029), falling back to title regex for nulls. Outputs: `output/fig_title_lengths.png`, `output/title-length-analysis.json`.
   136→
   137→Year coverage: 89.4% from `dates` column, 1.0% from title fallback, **9.6% no year** (429,097 titles).
   138→
   139→Overall distribution (all 4,477,780 titles, by `all_tokens` including stopwords; thresholds from [title-length-thresholds.md](title-length-thresholds.md)):
   140→
   141→| Category | Threshold | Count | % |
   142→|---|---|---|---|
   143→| Short | ≤ 4 tokens (p25) | 1,269,034 | 28.3% |
   144→| Medium | 5–14 tokens (p25–p75) | 2,110,610 | 47.1% |
   145→| Long | > 14 tokens (p75) | 1,098,136 | 24.5% |
   146→| **Median all_tokens** | | **8** | |
   147→| **Median content_tokens** | | **5** | |
   148→
   149→Distribution per 25-year bucket (N = 4,048,683 titles with year; 1500+):
   150→
   151→| Year bucket | Total | Short% | Medium% | Long% | Median all_t | Median con_t |
   152→|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
   153→| 1500–1524 | 12,209 | 14.9% | 39.8% | 45.3% | 13 | 7 |
   154→| 1525–1549 | 23,901 | 13.5% | 42.0% | 44.5% | 13 | 7 |
   155→| 1550–1574 | 33,802 | 15.3% | 42.2% | 42.6% | 12 | 6 |
   156→| 1575–1599 | 37,307 | 14.6% | 42.7% | 42.7% | 12 | 6 |
   157→| 1600–1624 | 57,887 | 14.7% | 36.7% | 48.6% | 14 | 7 |
   158→| 1625–1649 | 36,795 | 17.6% | 32.1% | 50.3% | 15 | 8 |
   159→| 1650–1674 | 56,317 | 16.4% | 34.6% | 49.0% | 14 | 7 |
   160→| 1675–1699 | 65,723 | 15.7% | 34.3% | 50.0% | 15 | 7 |
   161→| 1700–1724 | 112,587 | 17.0% | 37.7% | 45.3% | 13 | 6 |
   162→| 1725–1749 | 125,802 | 18.5% | 39.3% | 42.2% | 12 | 6 |
   163→| 1750–1774 | 183,051 | 22.1% | 44.3% | 33.6% | 10 | 5 |
   164→| 1775–1799 | 406,016 | 35.5% | 40.1% | 24.4% | 7 | 4 |
   165→| 1800–1824 | 195,586 | 25.5% | 41.9% | 32.5% | 9 | 5 |
   166→| 1825–1849 | 318,929 | 22.8% | 50.9% | 26.3% | 7 | 5 |
   167→| 1850–1874 | 364,664 | 24.4% | 49.0% | 26.7% | 9 | 5 |
   168→| 1875–1899 | 503,814 | 35.7% | 46.1% | 18.2% | 6 | 4 |
   169→| 1900–1924 | 624,305 | 38.4% | 49.4% | 12.2% | 6 | 4 |
   170→| 1925–1949 | 267,685 | 36.0% | 45.4% | 18.7% | 7 | 4 |
   171→| 1950–1974 | 107,850 | 36.5% | 43.1% | 20.4% | 7 | 4 |
   172→| 1975–1999 | 106,457 | 31.4% | 51.0% | 17.7% | 8 | 5 |
   173→| 2000–2024 | 400,569 | 8.9% | 62.2% | 28.9% | 11 | 6 |
   174→
   175→**Key findings:**
   176→- Pre-1750 titles are dominated by long strings: 42–50% long (>14 tokens), median `all_tokens` 12–15 — full ISBD-qualified bibliographic descriptions, not bare titles.
   177→- Post-1775: sharp shift — median drops to 6–9 tokens; long falls to 12–24%. Short (≤4) rises to 35–38% in the 1875–1949 period.
   178→- 2000–2024 reverses: only 9% short, 62% medium, 29% long — digital-born metadata with richer structured descriptions.
   179→- `content_tokens` (stopwords removed) runs consistently ~3 tokens below `all_tokens` (stopwords included) median — stable stopword overhead across all eras.
   180→- **Implication for SR-07 (gold set):** stratify by length as well as era; pre-1750 long-form records stress the NER model differently from the short modern majority. The 9.6% no-year group needs separate treatment — sample by `dc_type` or `silver_tier` instead.
   181→
   182→---
   183→
   184→## 3. FRBR label scope
   185→
   186→Labels are organised by FRBR level. The goal is to extract enough structure to distinguish Works, Expressions, and Manifestations in the KG.
   187→
   188→**Work** — the abstract intellectual creation
   189→
   190→| Label | What it marks | Typical ISBD / catalog cue |
   191→|---|---|---|
   192→| `TITLE` | Proper title | Before ` :` or ` /`; primary string |
   193→| `OTHER_TITLE` | Subtitle / other title information | After ` :` |
   194→| `PERSON` | Author / creator | After ` /`; "von", "hrsg. von", "ed. by" |
   195→
   196→**Expression** — a particular realization of the Work (language, translator, medium)
   197→
   198→| Label | What it marks | Typical ISBD / catalog cue |
   199→|---|---|---|



```

Not filtered by `dc:type`, provider, or era. Tokenization in the source notebook uses `spacy.load` (model unspecified in the trace).\n\n**Implication.** The corpus spans all eras and `dc:type` values — both long ISBD strings and short bare titles. ISBD coverage figures (20.2% ` :`, 0.8% ` /`) reflect this broad population.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Notable bump at 20 tokens (1.9% vs. 1.3% at 19 and 1.0% at 21) — likely a truncation artifact in the source data.\n\n**Threshold decision: quartiles (≤4 / 5–14 / >14)** — p25 = 4, p75 = 14, equal outer groups (~25% each). Title-length distribution by year\n\nScript: `scripts/analyse_title_lengths.py` — token counts from pre-computed `all_tokens` (includes stopwords) and `content_tokens` (stopwords removed); year from `dates` column (1400–2029), falling back to title regex for nulls.\n\nYear coverage: 89.4% from `dates` column, 1.0% from title regex fallback, **9.6% no year** (429,097 titles).\n\n![Title-length distribution by year](images/fig_title_lengths.png)\n\nOverall distribution (4,477,780 titles; `all_tokens` including stopwords):\n\n| Category | Threshold | Count | % |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Short | ≤ 4 tokens (p25) | 1,269,034 | 28.3% |\n| Medium | 5–14 tokens (p25–p75) | 2,110,610 | 47.1% |\n| Long | > 14 tokens (p75) | 1,098,136 | 24.5% |\n| **Median all_tokens** | | **8** | |\n| **Median content_tokens** | | **5** | |\n\nPer 25-year bucket (N = 4,048,683 titles with year; 1500+):\n\n| Year bucket | Total | Short% | Medium% | Long% | Median all_t | Median con_t |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| 1500–1524 | 12,209 | 14.9% | 39.8% | 45.3% | 13 | 7 |\n| 1525–1549 | 23,901 | 13.5% | 42.0% | 44.5% | 13 | 7 |\n| 1550–1574 | 33,802 | 15.3% | 42.2% | 42.6% | 12 | 6 |\n| 1575–1599 | 37,307 | 14.6% | 42.7% | 42.7% | 12 | 6 |\n| 1600–1624 | 57,887 | 14.7% | 36.7% | 48.6% | 14 | 7 |\n| 1625–1649 | 36,795 | 17.6% | 32.1% | 50.3% | 15 | 8 |\n| 1650–1674 | 56,317 | 16.4% | 34.6% | 49.0% | 14 | 7 |\n| 1675–1699 | 65,723 | 15.7% | 34.3% | 50.0% | 15 | 7 |\n| 1700–1724 | 112,587 | 17.0% | 37.7% | 45.3% | 13 | 6 |\n| 1725–1749 | 125,802 | 18.5% | 39.3% | 42.2% | 12 | 6 |\n| 1750–1774 | 183,051 | 22.1% | 44.3% | 33.6% | 10 | 5 |\n| 1775–1799 | 406,016 | 35.5% | 40.1% | 24.4% | 7 | 4 |\n| 1800–1824 | 195,586 | 25.5% | 41.9% | 32.5% | 9 | 5 |\n| 1825–1849 | 318,929 | 22.8% | 50.9% | 26.3% | 7 | 5 |\n| 1850–1874 | 364,664 | 24.4% | 49.0% | 26.7% | 9 | 5 |\n| 1875–1899 | 503,814 | 35.7% | 46.1% | 18.2% | 6 | 4 |\n| 1900–1924 | 624,305 | 38.4% | 49.4% | 12.2% | 6 | 4 |\n| 1925–1949 | 267,685 | 36.0% | 45.4% | 18.7% | 7 | 4 |\n| 1950–1974 | 107,850 | 36.5% | 43.1% | 20.4% | 7 | 4 |\n| 1975–1999 | 106,457 | 31.4% | 51.0% | 17.7% | 8 | 5 |\n| 2000–2024 | 400,569 | 8.9% | 62.2% | 28.9% | 11 | 6 |\n\n---\n\n## 4. Key findings\n\n- **Pre-1750:** 42–50% long (>14 tokens), median `all_tokens` 12–15. Full ISBD-qualified bibliographic descriptions, not bare titles.\n- **Post-1775:** sharp shift — median drops to 6–9 tokens; long falls to 12–24%; short rises to 35–38% in the 1875–1949 period.\n- **2000–2024 reverses:** only 9% short, 62% medium, 29% long — digital-born metadata with richer structured descriptions.\n- **Stopword overhead:** `content_tokens` runs ~3 tokens below `all_tokens` median consistently across all eras.\n- **Implication for SR-07 (gold set):** stratify by length as well as era. Pre-1750 long-form records stress the NER model differently from the short modern majority. The 9.6% no-year group needs separate treatment — sample by `dc_type` or `silver_tier` instead.\n"
}
```
