    27→| `check_generic_title_words.py` | Query DNB SPARQL endpoint to count GND Work records per GENERIC_TITLE_WORD; validates which words are high-collision and should be excluded from distinctive token selection |
    28→| `check_isbd_titles.py` | Analyse ISBD punctuation patterns in DF_DE_TITLES; reports per-pattern counts and sample titles |
    29→| `rate_isbd_fields.py` | Rate all 4.47M DF_DE_TITLES records for the presence of bibliographic fields (TITLE, PERSON, PUBLISHER, PLACE, YEAR, EDITION, SERIES, VOLUME) using ISBD rules; assigns silver_tier for NER training data selection; optionally writes per-pattern examples with DDB links |
    30→| `validate_heuristic_fields.py` | Sample 200 heuristic-tier records from isbd_field_ratings.csv for manual false-positive review; writes a review sheet CSV with DDB links and blank fp_fields / notes columns |
    31→| `explore_token_distribution.py` | Plot raw token-count distribution of `all_tokens` and `content_tokens` with percentile markers (p25/p50/p75/p90); used to identify data-driven short/medium/long thresholds; outputs `notes/images/fig_token_distribution.png` and `output/token-distribution.json` |
    32→| `analyse_title_lengths.py` | Plot title-length distribution (short/medium/long) per year bucket using `all_tokens` and `content_tokens`; year from `dates` column with title-string fallback; outputs `notes/images/fig_title_lengths.png` and `output/title-length-analysis.json` |
    33→| `validate_translator_disambiguation.py` | SR-04: sample 100 heuristic `f_person` records and apply translator/editor keyword heuristic to classify SoR text as TRANSLATOR / EDITOR / PERSON; writes `data/processed/translator_validation_sample.csv` for manual precision review |
    34→| `evaluate_translator_heuristic.py` | SR-04: evaluate keyword heuristic against manual `true_class` annotations in `translator_validation_sample.csv`; prints precision/recall/F1 for TRANSLATOR and EDITOR, confusion matrix, and false-negative detail |
    35→| `sr03_fp_review.py` | SR-03 false-positive review: reads `data/processed/heuristic_validation_sample.csv`, applies automated regex rules + per-row manual overrides to classify each active heuristic flag (f_year, f_other_title, f_person, f_person_compound, f_parallel, f_edition, f_publisher, f_series, f_volume) as TP or FP, and writes results back to `fp_fields` and `notes` columns |

Gold set annotators must not rely on the SoR position as a cue for the `PERSON` label in this stratum; author spans need to be identified from the opening name + credentials pattern (e.g. *Firstname Lastname, [role/title], [work title]*). This affects annotation guidelines and model evaluation: PERSON F1 on the pre-1750 stratum should be tracked separately from the modern stratum. See [silver-label-fp-review.md §5](ner/silver-label-fp-review.md#5-pre-1750-false-negatives--author-before-title) for examples.

```
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-applicability.md:197:| **Author before title** | Author name + credentials appear at the start of the string, before the work title. No ` /` present → `f_person = 0` even when an author is named | Requires name-before-title NER pattern; do not rely on `f_resp_person`. See [silver-label-fp-review.md §5](silver-label-fp-review.md#5-pre-1750-false-negatives--author-before-title) |
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating-adr.md:154:**Evidence:** SR-03 — [silver-label-fp-review.md](silver-label-fp-review.md)
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating-adr.md:184:**Evidence:** SR-04 — [translator-person-disambiguation.md](translator-person-disambiguation.md)
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:6:**ADR:** [isbd-field-rating-adr.md](isbd-field-rating-adr.md)
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:106:FP rates from SR-03 validation (200-record stratified sample). See [silver-label-fp-review.md](silver-label-fp-review.md).
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:123:SR-04 validation (100-record sample of `f_person = 1`, heuristic tier) found that ` /` fires on four distinct content types, not only personal name SoRs. A post-classification step is required before using `f_person` as a silver label. See [translator-person-disambiguation.md](translator-person-disambiguation.md).
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:215:**`f_year` at 14.6% vs. expected 20–30%.** The year regex `\b(?:1[4-9]\d{2}|20[012]\d)\b` covers 1400–2029. Lower coverage than expected reflects that most DDB records store the date in the `dates` column rather than the title string (89.4% have a year in `dates`; only 14.6% in the title). See [de-titles-distribution.md](de-titles-distribution.md).
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:219:**SR-03: heuristic tier FP rates (200-record sample).** `f_parallel` (~80% FP) and `f_edition` (~83% FP) are excluded from silver labels. `f_person` (~36% FP) requires SoR sub-classification post-filter. `f_year` (~6% FP) and `f_other_title` (~8% FP) are accepted. See [silver-label-fp-review.md](silver-label-fp-review.md).
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:221:**SR-04: SoR sub-classification (100-record sample of f_person).** Only 35% of `f_person` heuristic records are true individual-person SoRs. 41% are non-SoR false positives, 19% corporate bodies, 5% editors. Zero true translators detected. `f_person` must be sub-classified into `f_resp_person` / `f_resp_org` / `f_resp_editor` / `f_resp_other` before use as a silver label. See [translator-person-disambiguation.md](translator-person-disambiguation.md).
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:223:**SR-10: title length by era.** Pre-1750 titles are 42–50% long (>14 tokens); post-1775 shift to median 6–9 tokens; 2000–2024 reversal. `all_tokens` includes stopwords and punctuation (spaCy `de_core_news_sm`). See [de-titles-distribution.md](de-titles-distribution.md) and [title-length-thresholds.md](title-length-thresholds.md).
```
